verses.anvaya_skt: विश्वस्य कर्ता भुवनस्य गोप्ता ब्रह्मा देवानां प्रथमः सम्बभूव सः सर्वविद्याप्रतिष्ठां ब्रह्मविद्यां ज्येष्ठपुत्राय अथर्वाय प्राह ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: viśvasya kartā bhuvanasya goptā brahmā devānāṁ prathamaḥ sambabhūva saḥ sarvavidyāpratiṣṭhāṁ brahmavidyāṁ jyeṣṭhaputrāya atharvāya prāha ||
verses.auro_md: Brahma first of the Gods was born, the creator of all, the world's protector; he to Atharvan, his eldest son, declared the God-knowledge in which all sciences have their foundation.

glossary.meaning_en: in which all sciences have their foundation

verses.hindi_explain: देवगणों में प्रथम ब्रह्मा की उत्पत्ति हुई जो सम्पूर्ण विश्व के कर्ता एवं लोक के संरक्षक हैं; उन्होंने अपने ज्येष्ठ पुत्र अथर्वा के प्रति उस ब्रह्मविद्या का कथन किया जिसमें समस्त विद्याओं की प्रतिष्ठा है।
verses.meaning_en: Brahma first of the Gods was born, the creator of all, the world's protector; he to Atharvan, his eldest son, declared the Godknowledge in which all sciences have their foundation.
verses.verse_skt:
ब्रह्मा देवानां प्रथमः सम्बभूव विश्वस्य कर्ता भुवनस्य गोप्ता।
स ब्रह्मविद्यां सर्वविद्याप्रतिष्ठामथर्वाय ज्येष्ठपुत्राय प्राह ॥
verses.verse_tl:
brahmā devānāṁ prathamaḥ sambabhūva viśvasya kartā bhuvanasya goptā |
sa brahmavidyāṁ sarvavidyāpratiṣṭhāmatharvāya jyeṣṭhaputrāya prāha ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अथर्वणे ब्रह्मा  यं प्रवदेत तां ब्रह्मविद्यां अधर्वा पुरा अङ्गिरे उवाच सः भारद्वाजाय सत्य्वहाय प्राह। भारद्वाजः परावरां अङ्गिरसे  प्राह ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: atharvaṇe brahmā  yaṁ pravadeta tāṁ brahmavidyāṁ adharvā purā aṅgire uvāca saḥ bhāradvājāya satyvahāya prāha| bhāradvājaḥ parāvarāṁ aṅgirase ( prāha ) ||
verses.auro_md: The God-knowledge by Brahma declared to Atharvan, Atharvan of old declared to Angir; he to Satyavaha the Bharadwaja told it, the Bharadwaja to Angiras, both the higher and the lower knowledge.

glossary.meaning_en: both the higher and the lower knowledge

verses.hindi_explain: जिस ब्रह्मविद्या का ब्रह्मा ने अथर्वा के प्रति कथन किया था उसका कथन पुराकाल में अथर्वा ने अंगिर के प्रति किया; उसने भारद्वाज सत्यवह के प्रति किया; भारद्वाज ने परा एवं अपरा, दोनों विद्याओं का अंगिरस के प्रति कथन किया।
verses.meaning_en: The Godknowledge by Brahma declared to Atharvan, Atharvan of old declared to Angir; he to Satyavaha the Bharadwaja told it, the Bharadwaja to Angiras, both the higher and the lower knowledge.
verses.verse_skt:
अथर्वणे यां प्रवदेत ब्रह्माथर्वा तं पुरोवाचाङ्गिरे ब्रह्मविद्याम्‌।
स भारद्वाजाय सत्यवाहाय प्राह भारद्वाजोऽङ्गिरसे परावराम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
atharvaṇe yāṁ pravadeta brahmātharvā taṁ purovācāṅgire brahmavidyām |
sa bhāradvājāya satyavāhāya prāha bhāradvājo'ṅgirase parāvarām ||

verses.anvaya_skt: महाशालः शौनकः ह वै विधिवत् उपसन्नः अङ्गिरसं पप्रच्छ नु भगवः कस्मिन् विज्ञाते सर्वं इदं विज्ञातं भवति इति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: mahāśālaḥ śaunakaḥ ha vai vidhivat upasannaḥ aṅgirasaṁ papraccha nu bhagavaḥ kasmin vijñāte sarvaṁ idaṁ vijñātaṁ bhavati iti ||
verses.auro_md: Shaunaka, the great house-lord, came to Angiras in the due way of the disciple and asked of him, "Lord, by knowing what does all this that is become known?

glossary.meaning_en: in the due way of the disciple

verses.hindi_explain: महाशालाधिपति शौनक अंगिरस के पास विधिवत् शिष्य रूप में आये और उनसे पूछाː "हे भगवन्! क्या है जिसे जान लेने के पश्चात् यह सब कुछ ज्ञात हो जाता है?
verses.meaning_en: Shaunaka, the great house-lord, came to Angiras in the due way of the disciple and asked of him, “Lord, by knowing what does all this that is become known?”
verses.verse_skt:
शौनको ह वै महाशालोऽङ्गिरसं विधिवदुपसन्नः पप्रच्छ।
कस्मिन् नु भगवो विज्ञाते सर्वमिदं विज्ञातं भवतीति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
śaunako ha vai mahāśālo'ṅgirasaṁ vidhivadupasannaḥ papraccha |
kasmin nu bhagavo vijñāte sarvamidaṁ vijñātaṁ bhavatīti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सः तस्मै उवाच ह द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये इति ह ब्रह्मविदः वदन्ति स्म यत् परा च अपरा च एव ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: saḥ tasmai uvāca ha dve vidye veditavye iti ha brahmavidaḥ vadanti sma yat parā ca aparā ca eva ||
verses.auro_md: To him thus spoke Angiras: Twofold is the knowledge that must be known of which the knowers of the Brahman tell, the higher and the lower knowledge.

glossary.meaning_en: of which the knowers of the Brahman tell

verses.hindi_explain: अंगिरस ने उनसे यह कहाː दो प्रकार की विद्याऐं हैं जो जानने योग्य हैं, जिनके विषय में ब्रह्मविद्-ज्ञानी बताते हैं, परा तथा अपरा।
verses.meaning_en: To him thus spoke Angiras: Twofold is the knowledge that must be known of which the knowers of the Brahman tell, the higher and the lower knowledge.
verses.verse_skt: तस्मै स होवाच - - द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये इति ह स्म यद् ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति परा चैवापरा च ॥
verses.verse_tl: tasmai sa hovāca - - dve vidye veditavye iti ha sma yad brahmavido vadanti parā caivāparā ca ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तत्र अपरा ऋग्वेदः यजुर्वेदः सामवेदः अथर्ववेदः शिक्षा कल्पः व्याकरणं निरुक्तम्ं छन्दः ज्योतिषम् इति। अथ परा यया तत् अक्षरम् अधिगम्यते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tatra aparā ṛgvedaḥ yajurvedaḥ sāmavedaḥ atharvavedaḥ śikṣā kalpaḥ vyākaraṇaṁ niruktamṁ chandaḥ jyotiṣam iti| atha parā yayā tat akṣaram adhigamyate ||
verses.auro_md: Of which the lower, the Rig Veda and the Yajur Veda and the Sama Veda and the Atharva Veda, chanting, ritual, grammar, etymological interpretation, and prosody and astronomy. And then the higher by which is known the Immutable.
verses.hindi_explain: उसमें अपरा है, ऋग्वेद, यजुर्वेद, सामवेद, अथर्ववेद शिक्षा, कल्प, व्याकरण, निरुक्त, छन्द तथा ज्योतिष। और परा विद्या वह है जिससे 'अक्षर तत्त्व' का ज्ञान होता है।
verses.meaning_en: Of which the lower, the Rig Veda and the Yajur Veda and the Sama Veda and the Atharva Veda, chanting, ritual, grammar, etymological interpretation, and prosody and astronomy. And then the higher by which is known the Immutable.
verses.verse_skt:
तत्रापरा ऋग्वेदो यजुर्वेदः सामवेदोऽथर्ववेदः शिक्शा कल्पो व्याकरणं निरुक्तं छन्दो ज्योतिषमिति।
अथ परा यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tatrāparā ṛgvedo yajurvedaḥ sāmavedo'tharvavedaḥ śikśā kalpo vyākaraṇaṁ niruktaṁ chando jyotiṣamiti |
atha parā yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यत् तत् अद्रैश्यम् अग्राह्यम् अगोत्रम् अवर्णम् अचक्षुः श्रोत्रम्। तत् अपाणिपादं नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं तत् अव्ययं यत् धीराः भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yat tat adraiśyam agrāhyam agotram avarṇam acakṣuḥ śrotram| tat apāṇipādaṁ nityaṁ vibhuṁ sarvagataṁ susūkṣmaṁ tat avyayaṁ yat dhīrāḥ bhūtayoniṁ paripaśyanti ||
verses.auro_md: That the invisible, that the unseizable, without connections, without hue, without eye or ear, that which is without hands or feet, eternal, pervading, which is in all things and impalpable, that which is Imperishable, that which is the womb of creatures sages behold everywhere.

glossary.meaning_en: that which is without hands or feet
that which is the womb of creatures

verses.hindi_explain: 'वह' जो अदृश्य है, अग्राह्य है, सम्बन्धहीन (अगोत्र) है। अवर्ण है, चक्षु तथा श्रोत्र रहित है, जो अपाणिपाद (हाथ-पाँव रहित) है, नित्य है, विभु है, सर्वगत है, सब में ओतप्रोत है, अतिसूक्ष्म है, अव्यय है, जो समस्त प्राणियों की उत्पत्ति का उद्गम-स्थल (योनि) है, ज्ञानीजन (धीरजन) सर्वत्र उसका दर्शन करते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: That the invisible, that the unseizable, without connections, without hue, without eye or ear, that which is without hands or feet, eternal, pervading, which is in all things and impalpable, that which is Imperishable, that which is the womb of creatures sages behold everywhere.
verses.verse_skt:
यत् तदद्रेश्यमग्राह्यमगोत्रमवर्णमचक्षुःश्रोत्रं तदपाणिपादम्‌।
नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं तदव्ययं यद् भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति धीराः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yat tadadreśyamagrāhyamagotramavarṇamacakṣuḥśrotraṁ tadapāṇipādam |
nityaṁ vibhuṁ sarvagataṁ susūkṣmaṁ tadavyayaṁ yad bhūtayoniṁ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यथा ऊर्णनाभिः तन्तून् सृजते गृह्णते च यथा पृथिवीम् ओषधयः सम्भवन्ति यथा सतः पुरुषात् केशलोमानि सम्भवन्ति तथा इह अक्षरात् विश्वम् सम्भवति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yathā ūūrṇanābhiḥ ( tantūn ) sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivīm oṣadhayaḥ sambhavanti yathā sataḥ puruṣāt keśalomāni ( sambhavanti ) tathā iha akṣarāt viśvam sambhavati ||
verses.auro_md: As the spider puts out and gathers in, as herbs spring up upon the earth, as hair of head and body grow from a living man, so here all is born from the Immutable.

glossary.word_tl: hair of head and body grow

verses.hindi_explain: जिस प्रकार मकड़ी अपने (जाले का) सर्जन करती है तथा उसे अपने में समेट लेती है, जिस प्रकार पृथ्वी पर औषधियाँ उत्पन्न होती हैं, जिस प्रकार जीवित मनुष्य के शरीर के रोम तथा सिर के बाल बढ़ते हैं, उसी प्रकार 'अक्षर तत्त्व' से यही सब कुछ उत्पन्न होता है।
verses.meaning_en: As the spider puts out and gathers in, as herbs spring up upon the earth, as hair of head and body grow from a living man, so here all is born from the Immutable.
verses.verse_skt:
यथोर्णनाभिः सृजते गृह्णते च यथा पृथिव्यामोषधयः संभवन्ति।
यथा सतः पुरुषात्‌ केशलोमानि तथाऽक्षरात्‌ संभवतीह विश्वम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivyāmoṣadhayaḥ saṁbhavanti |
yathā sataḥ puruṣāt keśalomāni tathā'kṣarāt saṁbhavatīha viśvam ||

verses.anvaya_skt: ब्रह्म तपसा चीयते। ततः अन्नम् अभिजायते। अन्नात् प्राणः मनः सत्यं लोकाः  तथा  कर्मसु अमृतं च ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: brahma tapasā cīyate| tataḥ annam abhijāyate| annāt prāṇaḥ manaḥ satyaṁ lokāḥ ( tathā ) karmasu amṛtaṁ ca ||
verses.auro_md: Brahman grows by his energy at work, and then from Him is Matter born, and out of Matter life, and mind and truth and the worlds, and in works immortality.
verses.hindi_explain: 'ब्रह्म' तपःक्रिया से संवर्धित होता है, तत्पश्चात् 'उससे' 'अन्न' की उत्पत्ति होती है। 'अन्न' से प्राण, मन, सत्य तथा लोकों की, तथा कर्म में अमृतत्व की।
verses.meaning_en: Brahman grows by his energy at work, and then from Him is Matter born, and out of Matter life, and mind and truth and the worlds, and in works immortality.
verses.verse_skt:
तपसा चीयते ब्रह्म ततोऽन्नमभिजायते।
अन्नात्‌ प्राणो मनः सत्यं लोकाः कर्मसु चामृतम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tapasā cīyate brahma tato'nnamabhijāyate |
annāt prāṇo manaḥ satyaṁ lokāḥ karmasu cāmṛtam ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यः सर्वज्ञः  यः  सर्ववित् यस्य ज्ञानमयं तपः  तस्मात् एतत् ब्रह्म नाम रुपम् अन्नं च जायते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yaḥ sarvajñaḥ ( yaḥ ) sarvavit yasya jñānamayaṁ tapaḥ  tasmāt etat brahma nāma rupam annaṁ ca jāyate ||
verses.auro_md: He who is the Omniscient, the all-wise. He whose energy is all made of knowledge, from Him is born this that is Brahman here, this Name and Form and Matter.

glossary.meaning_en: energy is all made of knowledge

verses.hindi_explain: जो 'सर्वज्ञ' है, सर्वविद् है, 'वह' जिसका तप ज्ञानमय है, 'उससे' ही यह 'ब्रह्म', यह 'नाम' 'रूप' एवं 'अन्न' उत्पन्न होते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: He who is the Omniscient, the allwise, He whose energy is all made of knowledge, from Him is born this that is Brahman here, this Name and Form and Matter.
verses.verse_skt:
यः सर्वज्ञः सर्वविद् यस्य ज्ञानमयं तपः।
तस्मादेतद् ब्रह्म नाम रूपमन्नं च जायाते ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavid yasya jñānamayaṁ tapaḥ |
tasmādetad brahma nāma rūpamannaṁ ca jāyāte ||

verses.anvaya_skt: कवयः मन्त्रेषु यानि कर्माणि अपश्यन् तत् एतत् सत्यम्। त्रेतायाम् तानि बहुधा सन्ततानि। सत्यकामाः नियतम् तानि आचरथ। सुकृतस्य लोके वः एषः पन्थाः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: kavayaḥ mantreṣu yāni karmāṇi apaśyan tat etat satyam| tretāyām tāni bahudhā santatāni| satyakāmāḥ niyatam tāni ācaratha| sukṛtasya loke vaḥ eṣaḥ panthāḥ ||
verses.auro_md:
This is That, the Truth of things: works which the sages beheld in the Mantras[^1] were in the Treta[^2] manifoldly extended. Works do ye perform religiously with one passion for the Truth; this is your road to the heaven of good deeds.

[^1]: The inspired verses of the Veda.

[^2]: The second of the four ages.

glossary.meaning_en: this is That, the Truth of things
religiously with one passion for the Truth
to the heaven of good deeds

verses.hindi_explain: यह है 'वह' पदार्थों का 'सत्यतत्त्व'ː कवि-द्रष्टाओं ने मन्त्रों<sup>१</sup> में जिन कर्म को देखा, वे त्रेतायुग<sup>२</sup> में बहुधा विस्तारित हुए। उन कर्मों का एकनिष्ठ होकर 'सत्य' के लिए कामना करते हुए तुम नियमित आचरण करो; पुण्य कर्म-लोक (सुकृत-लोक) के लिए यही तुम्हारा पन्थ है।
verses.meaning_en: This is That, the Truth of things: works which the sages beheld in the Mantras were in the Treta manifoldly extended. Works do ye perform religiously with one passion for the Truth; this is your road to the heaven of good deeds.
verses.verse_skt:
तदेतत्‌ सत्यं मन्त्रेषु कर्माणि कवयो यान्यपश्यंस्तानि त्रेतायां बहुधा संततानि।
तान्याचरथ नियतं सत्यकामा एष वः पन्थाः सुकृतस्य लोके ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tadetat satyaṁ mantreṣu karmāṇi kavayo yānyapaśyaṁstāni tretāyāṁ bahudhā saṁtatāni |
tānyācaratha niyataṁ satyakāmā eṣa vaḥ panthāḥ sukṛtasya loke ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यदा समिद्धे हव्यवाहने अर्चिः लेलायते  तदा आज्यभागौ अन्तरेण आहुतीः प्रतिपादयेत् ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yadā samiddhe havyavāhane arciḥ lelāyate  tadā ājyabhāgau antareṇa āhutīḥ pratipādayet ||
verses.auro_md: When the fire of the sacrifice is kindled and the flame sways and quivers, then between the double pourings of butter cast therein with faith thy offerings.

glossary.meaning_en: the fire of the sacrifice is kindled
between the double pourings of butter

verses.hindi_explain: जब यज्ञाग्नि प्रज्वलित हो जाये ओर उसकी ज्वालाएँ ऊँची-ऊँची होकर लपलपाने लगें, तब घृत की दो आहुतियों (आज्यभागों) के बीच अपनी आहुतियों को श्रद्धा से समर्पित करो।
verses.meaning_en: When the fire of the sacrifice is kindled and the flame sways and quivers, then between the double pourings of butter cast therein with faith thy offerings.
verses.verse_skt:
यदा लेलायते ह्यर्चिः समिद्धे हव्यवाहने।
तदाज्यभागावन्तरेणाहुतीः प्रतिपादयेत्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yadā lelāyate hyarciḥ samiddhe havyavāhane |
tadājyabhāgāvantareṇāhutīḥ pratipādayet ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यस्य अग्निहोत्रम् अदर्शम् अपौर्णमासम् अचातुर्मास्यम् अनाग्रयणम् अतिथिवर्जितम् अहुतम् अवैश्वदेवम् अविधिना हुतम् च तस्य आसप्तमान् लोकान् हिनस्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yasya agnihotram adarśam apaurṇamāsam acāturmāsyam anāgrayaṇam atithivarjitam ahutam avaiśvadevam avidhinā hutam ca tasya āsaptamān lokān hinasti ||
verses.auro_md: For he whose altar-fires are empty of the new-moon offering and the full-moon offering, and the offering of the rains and the offering of the first fruits, or unfed, or fed without right ritual, or without guests or without the dues to the Vishwa-Devas, destroys his hope of all the seven worlds.

glossary.meaning_en: are empty of the newmoon offering
are empty of the fullmoon offering
are empty of the offering of the rains
are empty of the offering of the first fruits
without the dues to the VishwaDevas
hope of all the seven worlds

verses.hindi_explain: जिसकी यज्ञाग्नि (अग्निहोत्र) अमावस्या को दी जाने वाली आहुति से रहित है, पूर्णिमा को दी जाने वाली आहुति से रहित है, वर्षाकाल की (चातुर्मास्य) आहुति तथा प्रथम फल-अन्नादि के समय दी जाने वाली आहुति से रहित है, वह अहुत है अथवा अविधिपूर्वक हुत है अथवा अतिथि-यज्ञ-विहीन है, अथवा विश्व-देवों को दी जाने वाली आहुतियों से रहित है, वह अपने सातों लोकों की पुण्य-प्रत्याशा को नष्ट कर देता है।
verses.meaning_en: For he whose altarfires are empty of the newmoon offering and the fullmoon offering and the offering of the rains and the offering of the first fruits, or unfed, or fed without right ritual, or without guests or without the dues to the VishwaDevas, destroys his hope of all the seven worlds.
verses.verse_skt:
यस्याग्निहोत्रमदर्शमपौर्णमास-मचातुर्मास्यमनाग्रयणमतिथिवर्जितं च।
अहुतमवैश्वदेवमविधिना हुत-मासप्तमांस्तस्य लोकान्‌ हिनस्ति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yasyāgnihotramadarśamapaurṇamāsa-macāturmāsyamanāgrayaṇamatithivarjitaṁ ca |
ahutamavaiśvadevamavidhinā huta-māsaptamāṁstasya lokān hinasti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: काली कराली मनोजवा सुलोहिता सुधुम्रवर्णा स्फुलिङ्गिनी विश्वरूचि देवी च इति लेलायमानाः सप्त जिह्वाः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: kālī karālī manojavā sulohitā sudhumravarṇā sphuliṅginī viśvarūci devī ca iti lelāyamānāḥ sapta jihvāḥ ||
verses.auro_md: Kali, the black, Karali, the terrible, Manojava, thought-swift, Sulohita, blood-red, Sudhumravarna, smoke-hued, Sphulingini, scattering sparks, Vishwaruchi, the all-beautiful these are the seven swaying tongues of the fire.

glossary.meaning_en: these are the seven swaying tongues of the fire

verses.hindi_explain:
'काली-श्यामवर्णा, 'कराली'-भयंकरी, 'मनोजवा'-मन की गति के समान द्रुत वेगवाली, 'सुलोहिता'-रक्तवर्णा, 'सुधूम्रवर्णा'-धुएँ के सदृश वर्णवाली, 'स्फुलिङ्गिनी'-स्फुर्लिगों को बिखेरने वाली, 'विश्वरुचि'-सर्व-सुन्दरी, ये हैं अग्नि की लपलपाती सप्त जिह्वाएँ । 
<sup>१</sup>वेदों के मन्त्र।
<sup>२</sup>चतुर्युगों में द्वितीय युग।
verses.meaning_en: kälé karälé manojavä sulohitä sudhümravarëä sphuliìginé viçvarücé devé ca iti leläyamänäù sapta jihväù ||
verses.verse_skt:
काली कराली च मनोजवा च सुलोहिता या च सुधूम्रवर्णा।
स्फुलिङ्गिनी विश्वरुची च देवी लेलायमाना इति सप्त जिह्वाः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
kālī karālī ca manojavā ca sulohitā yā ca sudhūmravarṇā |
sphuliṅginī viśvarucī ca devī lelāyamānā iti sapta jihvāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यः भ्राजमानेषु एतेषु यथाकालम् आहुतयः आददायन् चरते तम् एताः सूर्यस्य रश्मयः  भूत्वा  यत्र एकः देवानाम् पतिः अधिवासः  तत्र  नयन्ति हि ।।
verses.anvaya_tl: yaḥ bhrājamāneṣu eteṣu yathākālam āhutayaḥ ādadāyan carate tam etāḥ sūryasya raśmayaḥ ( bhūtvā ) yatra ekaḥ devānām patiḥ adhivāsaḥ ( tatra ) nayanti hi ||
verses.auro_md: He who in these when they are blazing bright performs the rites, in their due season, him his fires of sacrifice take and they lead him, these rays of the sun, there where the Overlord of the Gods is the Inhabitant on high.

glossary.meaning_en: in these when they are blazing bright

verses.hindi_explain: इनके तीव्र जाज्वल्यमान हो जाने पर जो याचक यथोचित काल में यज्ञानुष्ठानों को करता है, उसे उसकी यज्ञाग्नि, ये सूर्य की रश्मियाँ वहां ले जाती हैं जहां देवों के 'अधीश्वर' ही ऊर्ध्वलोक के एकमात्र 'अधिवासी' हैं।
verses.meaning_en: He who in these when they are blazing bright performs the rites, in their due season, him his fires of sacrifice take and they lead him, these rays of the Sun, there where the Overlord of the gods is the Inhabitant on high.
verses.verse_skt:
एतेषु यश्चरते भ्राजमानेषु यथाकालं चाहुतयो ह्याददायन्‌।
तं नयन्त्येताः सूर्यस्य रश्मयो यत्र देवानां पतिरेकोऽधिवासः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
eteṣu yaścarate bhrājamāneṣu yathākālaṁ cāhutayo hyādadāyan |
taṁ nayantyetāḥ sūryasya raśmayo yatra devānāṁ patireko'dhivāsaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सुबर्चसः आहुतयः एहि एहि एषः वः सुकृतः पुण्यः ब्रह्मलोकः इति प्रियां वाचम् अभिवदन्त्यः अर्चयन्त्यः च तं यजमानं सूर्यस्य रश्मिभिः वहन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: subarcasaḥ āhutayaḥ ehi ehi eṣaḥ vaḥ sukṛtaḥ puṇyaḥ brahmalokaḥ iti priyāṁ vācam abhivadantyaḥ arcayantyaḥ ca taṁ yajamānaṁ sūryasya raśmibhiḥ vahanti ||
verses.auro_md: "Come with us", "Come with us", they cry to him, these luminous fires of sacrifice and they bear him by the rays of the sun speaking to him pleasant words of sweetness, doing him homage, "This is your holy world of Brahman and the heaven of your righteousness."

glossary.meaning_en: (cry to him) “Come with us”, “Come with us”
and the holy world of Brahman

verses.hindi_explain: ये दीप्तिमयी हुताग्नियाँ उस यजमान को ''आओ, हमारे साथ आओ'' पुकार कर कहती हैं तथा प्रियवाणी के द्वारा उसका अभिवादन करते हुए, उसकी पूजा अर्चना करते हुए, सूर्य की रश्मियों के माध्यम से उसका वहन करती हैं; "यही है तुम्हारा पुण्य ब्रह्मलोक, तुम्हारा सुकृत-लभ्य स्वर्ग।''
verses.meaning_en: “Come with us”, “Come with us”, they cry to him, these luminous fires of sacrifice, and they bear him by the rays of the Sun speaking to him pleasant words of sweetness, doing him homage, “This is your holy world of Brahman and the heaven of your righteousness.”
verses.verse_skt:
एह्येहीति तमाहुतयः सुवर्चसः सूर्यस्य रश्मिभिर्यजमानं वहन्ति।
प्रियां वाचमभिवदन्त्योऽर्चयन्त्य एष वः पुण्यः सुकृतो ब्रह्मलोकः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
ehyehīti tamāhutayaḥ suvarcasaḥ sūryasya raśmibhiryajamānaṁ vahanti |
priyāṁ vācamabhivadantyo'rcayantya eṣa vaḥ puṇyaḥ sukṛto brahmalokaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: एते हि अष्टादश यज्ञरूपाः प्लबाः अदृढाः येषु अवरं कर्म उक्त्वम्। ये मूढाः एतत् श्रेयः  इति  अभिनन्दन्ति  ते पुनः अपि जरामृत्युं यन्ति एव ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: ete hi aṣṭādaśa yajñarūpāḥ plabāḥ adṛḍhāḥ yeṣu avaraṁ karma uktvam| ye mūḍhāḥ etat śreyaḥ ( iti ) abhinandanti  te punaḥ api jarāmṛtyuṁ yanti eva ||
verses.auro_md: But frail are the ships of sacrifice, frail these forms of sacrifice, all the eighteen of them, in which are declared the lower works; fools are they who hail them as the highest good and they come yet again to this world of age and death.

glossary.meaning_en: to this world of age and death

verses.hindi_explain: किन्तु ये यज्ञरूपी नौकाएँ अतिजर्जर हैं, ये अठारह के अठारह ये अदृढ हैं जिनमें अवरकर्मो (निम्न-कर्मो) के विधान का कथन किया गया है; मूढ हैं वे जन, जो इनको श्रेय मानकर अभिनन्दन करते हैं, वे इस जरा-मृत्यु के लोक में पुनरावर्तन करते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: But frail are the ships of sacrifice, frail these forms of sacrifice, all the eighteen of them, in which are declared the lower works; fools are they who hail them as the highest good and they come yet again to this world of age and death.
verses.verse_skt:
प्लवा ह्येते अदृढा यज्ञरूपा अष्टादशोक्तमवरं येषु कर्म।
एतच्छ्रेयो येऽभिनन्दन्ति मूढा जरामृत्युं ते पुनरेवापि यन्ति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
plavā hyete adṛḍhā yajñarūpā aṣṭādaśoktamavaraṁ yeṣu karma |
etacchreyo ye'bhinandanti mūḍhā jarāmṛtyuṁ te punarevāpi yanti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अविद्यायाम् अन्तरे वर्तमानः स्वयं धीराः पण्डितं मन्यमानः जङ्घन्यमानाः मूढाः अन्धेन एव नीयामानाः अन्धाः यथा परियन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: avidyāyām antare vartamānaḥ svayaṁ dhīrāḥ paṇḍitaṁ manyamānaḥ jaṅghanyamānāḥ mūḍhāḥ andhena eva nīyāmānāḥ andhāḥ yathā pariyanti ||
verses.auro_md: They who dwell shut within the ignorance and they hold themselves for learned men thinking, "We, even we are the wise and the sages"—fools are they and they wander around beaten and stumbling like blind men led by the blind.
verses.hindi_explain: 'अविद्या' के अन्दर बन्द रहने वाले ये लोग जो स्वयं को विद्वान् मानकर सोचते हैː ''हम भी विद्वान् तथा पण्डित हैं'', वस्तुतः मूढ हैं, तथा वे उसी प्रकार चोटें तथा ठोकरें खाते हुए भटकते हैं जैसे अन्धे के द्वारा ले जाया गया अन्धा।
verses.meaning_en: They who dwell shut within the Ignorance and they hold themselves for learned men thinking “We, even we are the wise and the sages”-fools are they and they wander around beaten and stumbling like blind men led by the blind.
verses.verse_skt:
अविद्यायामन्तरे वर्तमानाः स्वयं धीराः पण्डितं मन्य७मानाः।
जङ्घन्यमानाः परियन्ति मूढा अन्धेनैव नीयमाना यथान्धाः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
avidyāyāmantare vartamānāḥ svayaṁ dhīrāḥ paṇḍitaṁ manya7mānāḥ |
jaṅghanyamānāḥ pariyanti mūḍhā andhenaiva nīyamānā yathāndhāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अविद्यायां बहुधा वर्तमानाः बालाः वयं कृतार्थाः इति अभिमन्यन्ति। यत् कर्मिणः रागात् न प्रवेदयन्ति   तेन क्षीणलोकाः आतुराः च्यवन्ते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: avidyāyāṁ bahudhā vartamānāḥ bālāḥ vayaṁ kṛtārthāḥ iti abhimanyanti| yat karmiṇaḥ rāgāt na pravedayanti   tena kṣīṇalokāḥ āturāḥ cyavante ||
verses.auro_md: They dwell in many bonds of the Ignorance, children thinking, "We have achieved our aim of Paradise"; for when the men of works are held by their affections, and arrive not at the Knowledge, then they are overtaken by anguish, then their Paradise wastes by enjoying and they fall from their heavens.

glossary.meaning_en: “We have achieved our aim of Paradise”
when the Paradise wastes by enjoying

verses.hindi_explain: ये 'अविद्या' के बहुविध बन्धनों में रहते हैं तथा बालकों के समान गर्ववशात् सोचते हैं, "हमने अपने 'स्वर्ग' के लक्ष्य को प्राप्त कर लिया''; क्योंकि जब कर्मनिष्ठ व्यक्ति अपनी आसक्ति (राग) के कारण 'ज्ञान' लाभ नहीं कर पाते तब वे दुःख तथा आतुरता के वश में हो जाते हैं, तब उनका 'स्वर्ग', भोग के कारण क्षीण हो जाता है तथा वे स्वयं अपने स्वर्ग से पतित हो जाते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: They dwell in many bonds of the Ignorance, children thinking, “We have achieved our aim of Paradise”; for when the men of works are held by their affections, and arrive not at the Knowledge, then they are overtaken by anguish, then their Paradise wastes by enjoying and they fall from their heavens.
verses.verse_skt:
अविद्यायां बहुधा वर्तमाना वयं कृतार्था इत्यभिमन्यन्ति बालाः।
यत्‌ कर्मिणो न प्रवेदयन्ति रागात्‌ तेनातुराः क्षीणलोकाश्च्यवन्ते ॥
verses.verse_tl:
avidyāyāṁ bahudhā vartamānā vayaṁ kṛtārthā ityabhimanyanti bālāḥ |
yat karmiṇo na pravedayanti rāgāt tenāturāḥ kṣīṇalokāścyavante ||

verses.anvaya_skt: इष्टापूर्तं वरिष्ठं मन्यमानाः प्रमूढाः अन्यत् श्रेयः  अस्ति इति  न वेदयन्ते ते सुकृते नाकस्य पृष्ठे अनुभूत्वा इमं लोकं हीनतरं वा विशन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: iṣṭāpūrtaṁ variṣṭhaṁ manyamānāḥ pramūḍhāḥ anyat śreyaḥ ( asti iti ) na vedayante te sukṛte nākasya pṛṣṭhe anubhūtvā imaṁ lokaṁ hīnataraṁ vā viśanti ||
verses.auro_md: Minds bewildered who hold the oblation offered and the well dug for the greatest righteousness and know not any other highest good, on the back of heaven they enjoy the world won by their righteousness and enter again this or even a lower world.

glossary.meaning_en: the oblation offered and the well dug
the world won by their righteousness

verses.hindi_explain: मोहग्रस्त ये लोग जो यज्ञयागादि कर्मों तथा कूपादि खनन के कार्यों को ही सर्वश्रेष्ठ (वरिष्ठ) सत्कर्म समझते हैं तथा अन्य किसी श्रेय को नहीं जानते, वे स्वर्ग-पृष्ठ में सुकृत-लोक की अनुभूति करके इस लोक में अथवा इससे भी हीनतर लोक में प्रवेश करते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: Minds bewildered who hold the oblation offered and the well dug for the greatest righteousness and know not any other highest good, on the back of heaven they enjoy the world won by their righteousness and enter again this or even a lower world.
verses.verse_skt:
इष्टापूर्तं मन्यमाना वरिष्ठं नान्यच्छ्रेयो वेदयन्ते प्रमूढाः।
नाकस्य पृष्ठे ते सुकृतेऽनुभूत्वेमं लोकं हीनतरं वा विशन्ति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
iṣṭāpūrtaṁ manyamānā variṣṭhaṁ nānyacchreyo vedayante pramūḍhāḥ |
nākasya pṛṣṭhe te sukṛte'nubhūtvemaṁ lokaṁ hīnataraṁ vā viśanti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: ये हि शान्ताः विद्वांसः भैक्षचर्यां चरन्तः अरण्ये तपःश्रद्धे उपवसन्ति  ते विरजाः  सन्तः  सूर्यद्वारेण यत्र सः अमृतः अव्ययात्मा पुरुषः  तत्र  प्रयान्ति हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: ye hi śāntāḥ vidvāṁsaḥ bhaikṣacaryāṁ carantaḥ araṇye tapaḥśraddhe upavasanti  te virajāḥ ( santaḥ ) sūryadvāreṇa yatra saḥ amṛtaḥ avyayātmā puruṣaḥ ( tatra ) prayānti hi ||
verses.auro_md: But they who in the forest follow after faith and self-discipline, calm and full of knowledge, living upon alms, cast from them the dust of their passions, and through the gate of the Sun they pass on there where is the Immortal, the Spirit, the Self undecaying and imperishable.

glossary.meaning_en: cast from them the dust of their passions,
through the gate of the Sun

verses.hindi_explain: किन्तु जो अरण्य में अपनी श्रद्धा एवं तपश्चर्या का पालन करते हुए शान्त-चित्त एवं ज्ञाननिष्ठ भाव से भिक्षावृत्ति के द्वारा जीवनयापन करते हैं, वे अपनी कामनाओं की धूलि (रज) से मुक्त होकर सूर्य के द्वार से निकलकर वहाँ पहुँच जाते हैं जहाँ 'अव्ययात्मा', 'अमृत-पुरुष' विद्यमान है।
verses.meaning_en: But they who in the forest follow after faith and self-discipline, calm and full of knowledge, living upon alms, cast from them the dust of their passions, and through the gate of the Sun they pass on there where is the Immortal, the Spirit, the Self undecaying and imperishable.
verses.verse_skt:
तपःश्रद्धे ये ह्युपवसन्त्यरण्ये शान्ता विद्वांसो भैक्ष्यचर्यां चरन्तः।
सूर्यद्वारेण ते विरजाः प्रयान्ति यत्रामृतः स पुरुषो ह्यव्ययात्मा ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tapaḥśraddhe ye hyupavasantyaraṇye śāntā vidvāṁso bhaikṣyacaryāṁ carantaḥ |
sūryadvāreṇa te virajāḥ prayānti yatrāmṛtaḥ sa puruṣo hyavyayātmā ||

verses.anvaya_skt: कर्मचितान् लोकान् परीक्ष ब्राह्मणः निर्वेदम् आयात्। कृतेन अकृतः न अस्ति तत् - विज्ञानार्थं  सः समित्पाणिः श्रोत्रियं ब्रह्मनिष्ठं गुरुम्  एव अभिगच्छेत् ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: karmacitān lokān parīkṣa brāhmaṇaḥ nirvedam āyāt| kṛtena akṛtaḥ na asti tat - vijñānārthaṁ  saḥ samitpāṇiḥ śrotriyaṁ brahmaniṣṭhaṁ gurum  eva abhigacchet ||
verses.auro_md:
The seeker of the Brahman, having put to the test the worlds piled up by works, arrives at world-distaste, for not by work done is reached He who is Uncreated.[^3] For the knowledge of That, let him approach, fuel in hand, a Guru, one who is learned in the Veda and is devoted to contemplation of the Brahman.

[^3]: Or, "He, the uncreated, lives not by that which is made." Literally, "not by the made (or, by that which is done) the Unmade (He who is uncreated)".

glossary.meaning_en: the worlds piled up by works
one who is learned in the Veda
devoted to contemplation of the Brahman

verses.hindi_explain: ब्रह्म-जिज्ञासु (ब्राह्मण) कर्म-संचित लोकों की परीक्षा करके संसार के फीकेपन (निर्वेद) का अनुभव करता है, क्योंकि कर्मों को करने से ही 'उस' की उपलब्धि नहीं हो सकती जो 'अकृत'<sup>३</sup> है। उस 'परतत्त्व' के ज्ञान के लिए वह (ब्राह्मण) हाथ में समिधा धारण करके वेदविद् (श्रोत्रिय) एवं ब्रह्मनिष्ठ गुरु के पास जाये । <sup>३</sup> अथवा, '''वह' जो असृष्ट है, सृष्ट पर आधारित नहीं है।"  शाब्दिक अनुवादː ''कृत के द्वारा (अथवा, बनाये गया के द्वारा) 'अकृत' ('यह' जो असृष्ट है) नहीं।"
verses.meaning_en: The seeker of the Brahman, having put to the test the worlds piled up by works, arrives at world-distaste, for not by work done is reached He who is Uncreated. For the knowledge of That, let him approach, fuel in hand, a Guru, one who is learned in the Veda and is devoted to contemplation of the Brahman.
verses.verse_skt:
परीक्ष्य लोकान्‌ कर्मचितान्‌ ब्राह्मणो निर्वेदमायान्नास्त्यकृतः कृतेन।
तद्विज्ञानार्थं स गुरुमेवाभिगच्छेत्‌ समित्पाणिः श्रोत्रियं ब्रह्मनिष्ठम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
parīkṣya lokān karmacitān brāhmaṇo nirvedamāyānnāstyakṛtaḥ kṛtena |
tadvijñānārthaṁ sa gurumevābhigacchet samitpāṇiḥ śrotriyaṁ brahmaniṣṭham ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सः  विद्वान् प्रशान्तचित्ताय शमन्विताय  सम्यक् उपसन्नाय तस्मै  जिज्ञासवे  येन सत्यम् अक्षरम् पुरुषं वेद  तां ब्रह्मविद्यां तत्वतः प्रोवाच ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: saḥ  vidvān praśāntacittāya śamanvitāya  samyak upasannāya tasmai ( jijñāsave ) yena satyam akṣaram puruṣaṁ veda  tāṁ brahmavidyāṁ tatvataḥ provāca ||
verses.auro_md: To him because he has taken entire refuge with him, with a heart tranquillised and a spirit at peace, that man of knowledge declares in its principles the science of the Brahman by which one comes to know the Immutable Spirit, the True and Real.

glossary.meaning_en: to the one with a heart tranquillised
to the one with a spirit at peace
him who has taken entire refuge with

verses.hindi_explain: उसके प्रति जो पूर्णरूप से शरण में आ गया है, जो शान्तचित्त हो गया है, जिसकी आत्मा में शान्ति विराजती है, विद्वान् उस ब्रह्मविद्या का तत्त्वतः प्रवचन करता है जिससे 'अक्षर पुरुष' को, 'परमसत्य' तथा 'सत्तत्त्व' को जाना जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: To him because he has taken entire refuge with him, with a heart tranquillised and a spirit at peace, that man of knowledge declares in its principles the science of the Brahman by which one comes to know the Immutable Spirit, the True and Real.
verses.verse_skt:
तस्मै स विद्वानुपसन्नाय सम्यक्‌ प्रशान्तचित्ताय शमान्विताय।
येनाक्षरं पुरुषं वेद सत्यं प्रोवाच तां तत्त्वतो ब्रह्मविद्याम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tasmai sa vidvānupasannāya samyak praśāntacittāya śamānvitāya |
yenākṣaraṁ puruṣaṁ veda satyaṁ provāca tāṁ tattvato brahmavidyām ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तत् एतत् सत्यं यथा सुदीप्तात् पावकात् सरूपाः विस्फुलिङ्गाः सहस्रशः प्रभवन्ते ह सौम्य विविधाः भावाः अक्षरात् प्रजायन्ते तत्र एव अपि यन्ति च ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tat etat satyaṁ yathā sudīptāt pāvakāt sarūpāḥ visphuliṅgāḥ sahasraśaḥ prabhavante  ( ha ) saumya vividhāḥ bhāvāḥ akṣarāt prajāyante  tatra eva api yanti ca ||
verses.auro_md: This is That, the Truth of things: as from one high-kindled fire thousands of different sparks are born and all have the same form of fire, so, O fair son, from the immutable manifold becomings are born and even into that they depart.

glossary.meaning_en: different sparks all having the same form of fire

verses.hindi_explain: हे सौम्य! यह है 'वह' पदार्थों का 'सत्यतत्त्व' जिस प्रकार सुदीप्त अग्नि से सहस्रों स्फुर्लिंग उत्पन्न होते हैं तथा वे सभी अग्नि के समान रूप वाले होते हैं, उसी प्रकार अक्षर-तत्त्व से अनेकानेक भावों का (सम्भूतियों का) उद्भव होता है तथा उसी में वे सब चले जाते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: This is That, the Truth of things: as from one highkindled fire thousands of different sparks are born and all have the same form of fire, so, O fair son, from the immutable manifold becomings are born and even into that they depart.
verses.verse_skt:
तदेतत्‌ सत्यं यथा सुदीप्तात्‌ पावकाद् विस्फुलिङ्गाः सहस्रशः प्रभवन्ते सरूपाः।
तथाक्षराद् विविधाः सोम्य भावाः प्रजायन्ते तत्र चैवापि यन्ति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tadetat satyaṁ yathā sudīptāt pāvakād visphuliṅgāḥ sahasraśaḥ prabhavante sarūpāḥ |
tathākṣarād vividhāḥ somya bhāvāḥ prajāyante tatra caivāpi yanti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: दिव्यः अमृतः अजः शुभ्रः पुरुषः सबाह्याभ्यन्तरः हि हि अप्राणः अमनाः  सः  अक्षरात् परतः ह ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: divyaḥ amṛtaḥ ajaḥ śubhraḥ puruṣaḥ sabāhyābhyantaraḥ hi hi aprāṇaḥ amanāḥ ( saḥ ) akṣarāt parataḥ ha ||
verses.auro_md: He, the divine, the formless Spirit, even He is the outward and the inward and he the Unborn; He is beyond life, beyond mind, luminous. Supreme beyond the immutable.
verses.hindi_explain: वह दिव्य अमूर्त 'पुरुष', वही बाह्य तथा आन्तर (सत्य) है एवं वह 'अज' है; वह प्राणों से परे (अप्राण) एवं मन से परे (अमन) है, वह शुभ्र ज्योतिर्मय एवं अक्षर से भी परे 'परमात्मतत्त्व' है।
verses.meaning_en: He, the divine, the formless Spirit, even he is the outward and the inward and he the Unborn; he is beyond life, beyond mind, luminous, Supreme beyond the immutable.
verses.verse_skt:
दिव्यो ह्यमूर्तः पुरुषः स बाह्याभ्यन्तरो ह्यजः।
अप्राणो ह्यमनाः शुभ्रो ह्यक्षरात्‌ परतः परः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
divyo hyamūrtaḥ puruṣaḥ sa bāhyābhyantaro hyajaḥ |
aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhro hyakṣarāt parataḥ paraḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: एतस्मात् प्राणः मनः सर्वेन्द्रियाणि खं वायौः ज्योतिः आपः विश्वस्य धारिणी पृथिवी च जायते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: etasmāt prāṇaḥ manaḥ sarvendriyāṇi khaṁ vāyauḥ jyotiḥ āpaḥ viśvasya dhāriṇīi pṛthivī ca jāyate ||
verses.auro_md: Life and mind and the senses are born from Him and the sky, and the wind, and light, and the waters and earth upholding all that is.
verses.hindi_explain: इसी 'परमात्म-तत्त्व' से प्राण, मन तथा समस्त इन्द्रियों का जन्म होता है; तथा आकाश, वायु, अग्नि, जल तथा सभी को धारण करने वाली पृथ्वी का भी जन्म होता है।
verses.meaning_en: Life and mind and the senses are born from Him and the sky, and the wind, and light, and the waters and earth upholding all that is.
verses.verse_skt:
एतस्माज्जायते प्राणो मनः सर्वेन्द्रियाणि च।
खं वायुर्ज्योतिरापः पृथिवी विश्वस्य धारिणी ॥
verses.verse_tl:
etasmājjāyate prāṇo manaḥ sarvendriyāṇi ca |
khaṁ vāyurjyotirāpaḥ pṛthivī viśvasya dhāriṇī ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अस्य च  अग्निः मूर्धा चन्द्रसूर्यौ चक्षुषी दिशः श्रोत्रे  विवृताः वेदाः वाक् वायुः प्राणः हृदयम् विश्वं अस्य पदभ्यां पृथिवी  जाता  एषः सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: ( asya ca ) agniḥ mūrdhā candrasūryau cakṣuṣī diśaḥ śrotre  vivṛtāḥ vedāḥ vāk vāyuḥ prāṇaḥ hṛdayam viśvaṁ asya padabhyāṁ pṛthivī ( jātā ) eṣaḥ sarvabhūtāntarātmā hi ||
verses.auro_md: Fire is the head of Him and His eyes are the Sun and Moon, the quarters His organs of hearing and the revealed Vedas are his voice, air is His breath, the universe is His heart, Earth lies at His feet. He is the inner Self in all beings.

glossary.meaning_en: is the inner Self in all beings

verses.hindi_explain: अग्नि 'उसका' मस्तक (मूर्धा) है, चन्द्रमा तथा सूर्य उसके नेत्र हैं, दिशाएँ उसकी श्रवणेन्द्रियाँ (श्रोत्र) हैं तथा प्रकट हुए वेद उसकी वाणी हैं, वायु उसका प्राण है, विश्व उसका हृदय है, उसके चरणों में पृथ्वी आसीन है, 'वह' समस्त उद्भूत सत्ताओं का 'अन्तरात्मा' है।
verses.meaning_en: Fire is the head of Him and his eyes are the Sun and Moon, the quarters his organs of hearing and the revealed Vedas are his voice, air is his breath, the universe is his heart, Earth lies at his feet. He is the inner Self in all beings.
verses.verse_skt:
अग्नीर्मूर्धा चक्षुषी चन्द्रसूर्यौ दिशः श्रोत्रे वाग्‌ विवृताश्च वेदाः।
वायुः प्राणो हृदयं विश्वमस्य पद्‌भ्यां पृथिवी ह्येष सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा ॥
verses.verse_tl:
agnīrmūrdhā cakṣuṣī candrasūryau diśaḥ śrotre vāg vivṛtāśca vedāḥ |
vāyuḥ prāṇo hṛdayaṁ viśvamasya padbhyāṁ pṛthivī hyeṣa sarvabhūtāntarātmā ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तस्मात् अग्निः  जातः  सूर्यः यस्य समिधः  सोमात् पर्जन्यः  सम्भवति   पृथिव्याम् ओषधयः  सम्भवन्ति   पुमान् योषितायां रेतः सिञ्चति   एवम् क्रमेण  पुरुषात् बह्वीः प्रजाः सम्प्रसूताः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tasmāt agniḥ ( jātaḥ ) sūryaḥ yasya samidhaḥ  somāt parjanyaḥ ( sambhavati )  pṛthivyām oṣadhayaḥ ( sambhavanti )  pumān yoṣitāyāṁ retaḥ siñcati  ( evam krameṇa ) puruṣāt bahvīḥ prajāḥ samprasūtāḥ ||
verses.auro_md: From Him is fire, of which the Sun is the fuel, then rain from the Soma, herbs upon the earth, and the male casts his seed into woman: thus are these many peoples born from the Spirit.
verses.hindi_explain: 'उसी' से अग्नि उत्पन्न होती है जिसकी समिधा है 'सूर्य', 'सोम' से वर्षा तथा पृथ्वी पर औषधियाँ (वनस्पतियाँ) उत्पन्न होती हैं, पुरुष स्त्री में वीर्य-सिंचन करता हैː इस प्रकार नानाविध प्रज्ञा उस 'परमात्म-तत्त्व' से उद्धूत हैं।
verses.meaning_en: From Him is fire, of which the Sun is the fuel, then rain from the Soma, herbs upon the earth, and the male casts his seed into woman: thus are these many peoples born from the Spirit.
verses.verse_skt:
तस्मादग्निः समिधो यस्य सूर्यः सोमात्‌ पर्जन्य ओषधयः पृथिव्याम्‌।
पुमान्‌ रेतः सिञ्चति योषितायां बह्वीः प्रजाः पुरुषात्‌ संप्रसूताः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tasmādagniḥ samidho yasya sūryaḥ somāt parjanya oṣadhayaḥ pṛthivyām |
pumān retaḥ siñcati yoṣitāyāṁ bahvīḥ prajāḥ puruṣāt saṁprasūtāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तस्मात् ऋचः सामानि यजुंषि दीक्षाः  सर्वे यज्ञाः च क्रतवः दक्षिणाः  च सम्वत्सरः यजमानः लोकाः च  उत्पन्नाः  यत्र सोमः पवते यत्र सूर्यः  पवते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tasmāt ṛcaḥ sāmāni yajuṁṣi dīkṣāḥ  sarve yajñāḥ ca kratavaḥ dakṣiṇāḥ  ca samvatsaraḥ yajamānaḥ lokāḥ ca ( utpannāḥ ) yatra somaḥ pavate yatra sūryaḥ ( pavate )
verses.auro_md: From Him are the hymns of the Rig-veda, the Sama and the Yajur, initiation, and all sacrifices and works of sacrifice, and dues given, the year and the giver of the sacrifice and the worlds, on which the moon shines and the sun.

glossary.meaning_en: the hymns of the Rig Veda

verses.hindi_explain: उसी 'परमात्म-तत्त्व' से ऋग्वेद की, सामवेद तथा यजुर्वेद की ऋचाएँ तथा मन्त्रगान हैं, दीक्षाएँ, समस्त यज्ञ तथा योग-कर्म और दान-दक्षिणाएँ हैं, उसी से संवत्सर हैं, यजमान हैं, लोक-लोकान्तर हैं जिनमें चन्द्रमा तथा सूर्य प्रकाश फैलाते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: From Him are the hymns of the Rig Veda, the Sama and the Yajur, initiation, and all sacrifices and works of sacrifice, and dues given, the year and the giver of the sacrifice and the worlds, on which the moon shines and the sun.
verses.verse_skt:
तस्मादृचः साम यजूंषि दीक्षा यज्ञाश्च सर्वे क्रतवो दक्षिणाश्च।
संवत्सरश्च यजमानश्च लोकाः सोमो यत्र पवते यत्र सूर्यः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tasmādṛcaḥ sāma yajūṁṣi dīkṣā yajñāśca sarve kratavo dakṣiṇāśca |
saṁvatsaraśca yajamānaśca lokāḥ somo yatra pavate yatra sūryaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तस्मात् च देवाः बहुधा सम्प्रसूताः साध्याः मनुष्याः पशवः वयांसि प्राणापानौ  ब्रीहियवौ तपः च श्रद्धा सत्यं ब्रह्मचर्यम्   विधि च  सम्प्रसूताः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tasmāt ca devāḥ bahudhā samprasūtāḥ sādhyāḥ manuṣyāḥ paśavaḥ vayāṁsi prāṇāpānau  brīhiyavau tapaḥ ca śraddhā satyaṁ brahmacaryam   vidhi ca ( samprasūtāḥ ) ||
verses.auro_md: And from Him have issued many gods, and demi-gods and men and beasts and birds, the main breath and downward breath, and rice and barley, and askesis and faith and Truth, and chastity and rule of right practice.

glossary.meaning_en: the main breath and downward breath

verses.hindi_explain: तथा 'उसी' से अनेकानेक देवगण उत्पन्न हुए हैं, उसी से साधुगण, मनुष्य तथा पशु-पक्षी उत्पन्न हुए हैं, प्राण तथा अपान वायु, धान तथा जौ (अन्न), तप, श्रद्धा, सत्य, ब्रह्मचर्य तथा विधि-विधानों का प्रादुर्भाव 'उसी' से हुआ है।
verses.meaning_en: And from Him have issued many gods, and demigods and men and beasts and birds, the main breath and downward breath, and rice and barley, and askesis and faith and Truth, and chastity and rule of right practice.
verses.verse_skt:
तस्माच्च देवा बहुधा संप्रसूताः साध्या मनुष्याः पशवो वयांसि।
प्राणापानौ व्रीहियवौ तपश्च श्रद्ध सत्यं ब्रह्मचर्यं विधिश्च ॥
verses.verse_tl:
tasmācca devā bahudhā saṁprasūtāḥ sādhyā manuṣyāḥ paśavo vayāṁsi |
prāṇāpānau vrīhiyavau tapaśca śraddha satyaṁ brahmacaryaṁ vidhiśca ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तस्मात् सप्त प्राणाः सप्त अर्चिषः  सप्त समिधा  होमाः इमे सप्त लोकाः प्रभवन्ति सप्त सप्त येषु निहिताः गुहाशयाः प्राणाः चरन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tasmāt sapta prāṇāḥ sapta arciṣaḥ  sapta samidhā  homāḥ ime sapta lokāḥ prabhavanti sapta sapta yeṣu nihitāḥ guhāśayāḥ prāṇāḥ caranti ||
verses.auro_md: The seven breaths are born from Him and the seven lights and kinds of fuel and the seven oblations and these seven worlds in which move the life-breaths set within with the secret heart for their dwelling-place, seven and seven.

glossary.meaning_en: the seven kinds of fuel and the seven oblations
with the secret heart for their dwelling place

verses.hindi_explain: 'उसी' से सप्त प्राणों का जन्म हुआ है, सप्त ज्वालाएँ, विभिन्न समिधाएँ सप्त होम तथा ये सन्त लोक जिनमें प्राण हृदय-गुहा को अपना  बना कर विचरण करते हैं, 'उसी' से उत्पन्न हैं; सभी सात-सात के समूहों में हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The seven breaths are born from Him and the seven lights and kinds of fuel and the seven oblations and these seven worlds in which move the lifebreaths set within with the secret heart for their dwellingplace, seven and seven.
verses.verse_skt:
सप्त प्राणाः प्रभवन्ति तस्मात्‌ सप्तार्चिषः समिधः सप्त होमाः।
सप्त इमे लोका येषु चरन्ति प्राणा गुहाशया निहिताः सप्त सप्त ॥
verses.verse_tl:
sapta prāṇāḥ prabhavanti tasmāt saptārciṣaḥ samidhaḥ sapta homāḥ |
sapta ime lokā yeṣu caranti prāṇā guhāśayā nihitāḥ sapta sapta ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अतः सर्वे समुद्राः गिरयः च  उत्पन्नाः  अस्मात् सर्वरूपाः सिन्धवः स्यन्दते। अतः च सर्वाः ओषधयः रसः च  सम्भवन्ति   येन एषः अन्तरात्मा भूतैः  परिबेष्ठितः  तिष्ठते हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: ataḥ sarve samudrāḥ girayaḥ ca ( utpannāḥ ) asmāt sarvarūpāḥ sindhavaḥ syandate| ataḥ ca sarvāḥ oṣadhayaḥ rasaḥ ca ( sambhavanti )  yena eṣaḥ antarātmā bhūtaiḥ ( paribeṣṭhitaḥ ) tiṣṭhate hi ||
verses.auro_md: From Him are the oceans and all these mountains and from Him flow rivers of all forms, and from Him are all plants, and sensible delight which makes the soul to abide with the material elements.
verses.hindi_explain: 'उसी' से सारे समुद्र तथा ये सारे पर्वत, ये सब प्रकार की नदियाँ 'उसी' से प्रवाहित होती हैं; 'उसी' से समस्त वनस्पतियां तथा रसादि हैं जिनकी रसानुभूति से यह अन्तरात्मा भौतिक तत्त्वों के साथ निवास करती है।
verses.meaning_en: From Him are the oceans and all these mountains and from Him flow rivers of all forms, and from Him are all plants, and sensible delight which makes the soul to abide with the material elements.
verses.verse_skt:
अतः समुद्रा गिरयश्च सर्वेऽस्मात्‌ स्यन्दन्ते सिन्धवः सर्वरूपाः।
अतश्च सर्वा ओषधयो रसाश्च येनैष भूतैस्तिष्ठते ह्यन्तरात्मा ॥
verses.verse_tl:
ataḥ samudrā girayaśca sarve'smāt syandante sindhavaḥ sarvarūpāḥ |
ataśca sarvā oṣadhayo rasāśca yenaiṣa bhūtaistiṣṭhate hyantarātmā ||

verses.anvaya_skt: इदं विश्वं कर्म तपः  च  पुरुषः एव गुहायाम् निहितं परामृतम् एतत् ब्रह्म यः वेद  हे  सौम्य सः अविद्याग्रन्थिं इह विकिरति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: idaṁ viśvaṁ karma tapaḥ ( ca ) puruṣaḥ eva guhāyām nihitaṁ parāmṛtam etat brahma yaḥ veda ( he ) saumya saḥ avidyāgranthiṁ iha vikirati ||
verses.auro_md: The Spirit is all this universe; he is works and askesis and the Brahman, supreme and immortal. O fair son, he who knows this hidden in the secret heart, scatters even here in this world the knot of the Ignorance.
verses.hindi_explain: यह 'पुरुष-तत्त्व' ही सम्पूर्ण विश्व है; 'वही' कर्म है, 'वही' तप है  तथा 'वही' परम तथा अमृतरूप 'ब्रह्म' है। हे सौम्य, जो हृद्गुहा में निहित इस तत्त्व को जानता है वह यहीं, इसी लोक में अविद्या-ग्रन्थि का उच्छेदन कर देता है।
verses.meaning_en: The Spirit is all this universe; He is works and askesis and the Brahman, supreme and immortal. O fair son, he who knows this hidden in the secret heart, scatters even here in this world the knot of the Ignorance.
verses.verse_skt:
पुरुष एवेदं विश्वं कर्म तपो ब्रह्म परामृतम्‌।
एतद्यो वेद निहितं गुहायां सोऽविद्याग्रन्थिं विकिरतीह सोम्य ॥
verses.verse_tl:
puruṣa evedaṁ viśvaṁ karma tapo brahma parāmṛtam |
etadyo veda nihitaṁ guhāyāṁ so'vidyāgranthiṁ vikiratīha somya ||

verses.anvaya_skt: अविः सन्निहितं गुहाचरं नाम यत् महत् पदम् अत्र एजत् प्राणत् निमिषत् च एतत्  सर्वं  समर्पितम् एतत् जानथ यत् सत् असत् वरेण्यम् वरिष्ठं  तथा  प्रजानां विज्ञानात् परम् ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: aviḥ sannihitaṁ guhācaraṁ nāma yat mahat padam atra ejat prāṇat nimiṣat ca etat ( sarvaṁ ) samarpitam etat jānatha yat sat asat vareṇyam variṣṭhaṁ ( tathā ) prajānāṁ vijñānāt param ||
verses.auro_md: Manifested, it is here set close within, moving in the secret heart, this is the mighty foundation and into it is consigned all that moves and breathes and sees. This that is that great foundation here, know, as the Is and Is-not, the supremely desirable, greatest and the Most High, beyond the knowledge of creatures.

glossary.meaning_en: it is here set close within
this is the mighty foundation here
into it is consigned This that is
know this, the mighty foundation here

verses.hindi_explain: स्वयं आविर्भूत परम तत्त्व यहाँ सन्निहित है, यह हृद्गुहा में विचरने वाला महान् पद है, इसमें ही यह सब समर्पित है जो गतिमान् है, प्राणवान् है तथा जो दृष्टिमान् है। यह जो यही महान् पद है, उसको ही 'सत्' तथा 'असत्' जानो, जो परम वरेण्य है, महत्तम एवं 'सर्वोच्च' (वरिष्ठ) है, तथा जो प्राणियों (प्रजाओं) के ज्ञान से परे है।
verses.meaning_en: Manifested, it is here set close within, moving in the secret heart, this is the mighty foundation and into it is consigned all that moves and breathes and sees. This that is that great foundation here, know, as the Is and Is not, the supremely desirable, greatest and the Most High, beyond the knowledge of creatures.
verses.verse_skt:
आविः सन्निहितं गुहाचरं नाम महत् पदमत्रैतत्‌ समर्पितम्‌।
एजत् प्राणन्निमिषच्च यदेतज्जानथ सदस-द्वरेण्यं परं विज्ञानाद्यद्वरिष्ठं प्रजानाम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
āviḥ sannihitaṁ guhācaraṁ nāma mahat padamatraitat samarpitam |
ejat prāṇannimiṣacca yadetajjānatha sadasa-dvareṇyaṁ paraṁ vijñānādyadvariṣṭhaṁ prajānām ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यत् अर्चिमत् यत् अणुभ्यः  अपि  अणु च यस्मिन् लोकाः लोकिनः च निहिताः तत् एतत् अक्षरं ब्रह्म सः प्राणः  तत् उ वाक् मनः तत् एतत् सत्यम् तत् अमृतम् तत्  मनसा  वेद्धव्यं सौम्य  तत्  विद्धि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yat arcimat yat aṇubhyaḥ ( api ) aṇu ca yasmin lokāḥ lokinaḥ ca nihitāḥ tat etat akṣaraṁ brahma saḥ prāṇaḥ  tat u vāk manaḥ tat etat satyam tat amṛtam tat ( manasā ) veddhavyaṁ saumya ( tat ) viddhi ||
verses.auro_md: That which is the Luminous, that which is smaller than the atoms, that in which are set the worlds and their peoples, That is This,—it is Brahman immutable: life is That, it is speech and mind. That is This, the True and Real, it is That which is immortal: it is into That that thou must pierce, O fair son, into That penetrate.

glossary.meaning_en: and that which is smaller than the atoms
and the peoples of the worlds
it is That which is immortal
it is into That that thou must pierce

verses.hindi_explain: यह जो 'ज्योतिर्मान्' है, जो अणुओं से भी सूक्ष्मतर है, जिसके अन्दर समस्त लोक-लोकान्तर एवं उनके लोकवासी सन्निहित हैं, 'वही' है 'यह'-यह अक्षर 'ब्रह्म' प्राणतत्त्व 'वही' है, 'वही' वाणी तथा मन है। 'वही' है 'यह' 'परम सत्य' तथा 'सत्तत्त्व', 'वही' है अमृत तत्त्व तुम्हारे द्वारा 'वही' है वेधनीय, है सौम्य! 'उसी' का वेधन करो (उसमें प्रवेश करो)।
verses.meaning_en: That which is the Luminous, that which is smaller than the atoms, that in which are set the worlds and their peoples, That is This,-it is Brahman immutable: life is That, it is speech and mind. That is This, the True and Real, it is That which is immortal: it is into That that thou must pierce, O fair son, into That penetrate.
verses.verse_skt:
यदर्चिमद्यदणुभ्योऽणु च यस्मिंल्लोका निहिता लोकिनश्च।
तदेतदक्षरं ब्रह्म स प्राणस्तदु वाङ् मनः तदेतत् सत्यं तदमृतं तद् वेद्धव्यं सोम्य विद्धि ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yadarcimadyadaṇubhyo'ṇu ca yasmiṁllokā nihitā lokinaśca |
tadetadakṣaraṁ brahma sa prāṇastadu vāṅ manaḥ tadetat satyaṁ tadamṛtaṁ tad veddhavyaṁ somya viddhi ||

verses.anvaya_skt: औपनिषदं महास्त्रं धनुः गृहित्वा उपासानिशितम् हि शरं सन्धयीत सौम्य तद्भावगतेन  चेतसा आयम्य तत् एव अक्षरं लक्ष्यं विद्धि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: aupaniṣadaṁ mahāstraṁ dhanuḥ gṛhitvā upāsāniśitam hi śaraṁ sandhayīta saumya tadbhāvagatena  cetasā āyamya tat eva akṣaraṁ lakṣyaṁ viddhi ||
verses.auro_md: Take up the bow of the Upanishad, that mighty weapon, set to it an arrow sharpened by adoration, draw the bow with a heart wholly devoted to the contemplation of That, and O fair son, penetrate into That as thy target, even into the Immutable.

glossary.meaning_en: with a heart wholly devoted to the contemplation of That

verses.hindi_explain: उपनिषदीय धनुष रूपी महाअश्व को ग्रहण करो, उपासना के द्वारा सुतीक्ष्ण किये हुए बाण को उस पर स्थापित करो, उस 'परतत्त्व' में चित्त को पूर्णतया एकनिष्ठ करके धनुष को खींचो तथा हे सौम्य! उस 'अक्षर ब्रह्म' को लक्ष्य करके, 'उसे' वेधो।
verses.meaning_en: Take up the bow of the Upanishad, that mighty weapon, set to it an arrow sharpened by adoration, draw the bow with a heart wholly devoted to the contemplation of That, and O fair son, penetrate into That as thy target, even into the Immutable.
verses.verse_skt:
धनुर्गृहीत्वौपनिषदं महास्त्रं शरं ह्युपासानिशितं सन्धयीत।
आयम्य तद् भावगतेन चेतसा लक्श्यं तदेवाक्षरं सोम्य विद्धि ॥
verses.verse_tl:
dhanurgṛhītvaupaniṣadaṁ mahāstraṁ śaraṁ hyupāsāniśitaṁ sandhayīta |
āyamya tad bhāvagatena cetasā lakśyaṁ tadevākṣaraṁ somya viddhi ||

verses.anvaya_skt: प्रणवः धनुः शरः हि आत्मा ब्रह्म तत् लक्ष्यम् उच्यते अप्रमत्तेन  तत् लक्ष्यं  वेद्धव्यं शरवत् तन्मयः भवेत् ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: praṇavaḥ dhanuḥ śaraḥ hi ātmā brahma tat lakṣyam ucyate apramattena ( tat lakṣyaṁ ) veddhavyaṁ śaravat tanmayaḥ bhavet ||
verses.auro_md: OM is the bow and the soul is the arrow, and That, even the Brahman, is spoken of as the target. That must be pierced with an unfaltering aim; one must be absorbed into That as an arrow is lost in its target.

glossary.meaning_en: as an arrow (lost in its target)

verses.hindi_explain: ॐ (प्रणव) है धनुष तथा आत्मा है बाण, और 'वह', अर्थात् 'ब्रह्म' को लक्ष्य के रूप में कहा गया है। 'उसका' प्रमाद रहित होकर वेधन करना चाहिये; जिस प्रकार शर अर्थात् बाण अपने लक्ष्य में विलुप्त हो जाता है उसी प्रकार मनुष्य को 'उस' में (ब्रह्म में) तन्मय हो जाना चाहिये।
verses.meaning_en: OM is the bow and the soul is the arrow, and That, even the Brahman, is spoken of as the target. That must be pierced with an unfaltering aim; one must be absorbed into That as an arrow is lost in its target.
verses.verse_skt:
प्रणवो धनुः शरो ह्यात्मा ब्रह्म तल्लक्ष्यमुच्यते।
अप्रमत्तेन वेद्धव्यं शरवत्‌ तन्मयो भवेत्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
praṇavo dhanuḥ śaro hyātmā brahma tallakṣyamucyate |
apramattena veddhavyaṁ śaravat tanmayo bhavet ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यस्मिन् द्यौः पृथिवी अन्तरिक्षं च सर्वैः प्राणैः सह मनः च ओतं तम् एकं आत्मानम्  इति  एव जानथ अन्याः वाचः विमुञ्चत। एषः अमृतस्य सेतुः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yasmin dyauḥ pṛthivī antarikṣaṁ ca sarvaiḥ prāṇaiḥ saha manaḥ ca otaṁ tam ekaṁ ātmānam ( iti ) eva jānatha anyāḥ vācaḥ vimuñcata| eṣaḥ amṛtasya setuḥ ||
verses.auro_md: He in whom are inwoven heaven and earth and the mid-region, and mind with all the life-currents, Him know to be the one Self; other words put away from you: this is the bridge to immortality.
verses.hindi_explain: 'वह', जिसमें द्युलोक, पृथ्वी एवं अन्तरिक्ष, मन तथा प्राण की समस्त गलियां अन्तर्भूत हैं, ओत-प्रोत हैं, 'उसको' तुम एकमेव 'आत्मरूप' जानो; इससे भिन्न अन्य वचनों का सर्वथा त्याग कर दो, यही है अमृत का सेतु।
verses.meaning_en: He in whom are inwoven heaven and earth and the midregion, and mind with all the life currents, Him know to be the one Self; other words put away from you: this is the bridge to immortality.
verses.verse_skt:
यस्मिन्‌ द्यौः पृथिवी चान्तरिक्षमोतं मनः सह प्राणैश्च सर्वैः।
तमेवैकं जानथ आत्मानमन्या वाचो विमुञ्चथामृतस्यैष सेतुः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yasmin dyauḥ pṛthivī cāntarikṣamotaṁ manaḥ saha prāṇaiśca sarvaiḥ |
tamevaikaṁ jānatha ātmānamanyā vāco vimuñcathāmṛtasyaiṣa setuḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: रथनाभौ अराः इव यत्र नाड्यः संहताः बहुधा जायमानः स एषः  आत्मा  अन्तः चरते आत्मानं ॐ इति एवं ध्यायथ वः तमसः परस्तात् पाराय स्वस्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: rathanābhau arāḥ iva yatra nāḍyaḥ saṁhatāḥ bahudhā jāyamānaḥ sa eṣaḥ ( ātmā ) antaḥ carate ātmānaṁ om iti evaṁ dhyāyatha vaḥ tamasaḥ parastāt pārāya svasti ||
verses.auro_md: Where the nerves are brought close together like the spokes in the nave of a chariot-wheel, this is He that moves within,—there is He manifoldly born. Meditate on the Self as OM and happy be your passage to the other shore beyond the darkness.

glossary.meaning_en: in the nave of a chariot-wheel

verses.hindi_explain: रथ के चक्र की नाभि में जुडे़ हुए अरों की भाँति जहाँ नाड़ियाँ एकत्रित हो जाती हैं, 'वह' ही है जो अन्तर में विचरण करता है,- 'वही' बहुविध रूप में जन्म लेता है। 'आत्मा' का 'ओम्', के रूप में ध्यान करो, तथा अन्धतमस् से परे उस पार जाने का तुम्हारा मार्ग मंगलमय (स्वस्ति) हो।
verses.meaning_en: Where the nerves are brought close together like the spokes in the nave of a chariot-wheel, this is He that moves within, -there is He manifoldly born. Meditate on the Self as OM and happy be your passage to the other shore beyond the darkness.
verses.verse_skt:
अरा इव रथनाभौ संहता यत्र नाड्यः स एषोऽन्तश्चरते बहुधा जायमानः।
ओमित्येवं ध्यायथ आत्मानं स्वस्ति वः पाराय तमसः परस्तात्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
arā iva rathanābhau saṁhatā yatra nāḍyaḥ sa eṣo'ntaścarate bahudhā jāyamānaḥ |
omityevaṁ dhyāyatha ātmānaṁ svasti vaḥ pārāya tamasaḥ parastāt ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यः सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित् भुवि यस्य एषः महिमा एषः आत्मा  दिव्ये ब्रह्मपुरे व्योम्नि प्रतिष्ठितः हि ।
verses.anvaya_tl: yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavit bhuvi yasya eṣaḥ mahimā eṣaḥ ātmā  divye brahmapure vyomni pratiṣṭhitaḥ hi |
verses.auro_md: The Omniscient, the All-wise, whose is this might and majesty upon the earth, is this self enthroned in the Divine city of the Brahman, in his ethereal heaven.

glossary.meaning_en: in the divine city of the Brahman

verses.hindi_explain: जो 'सर्वज्ञ' है 'सर्वविद्' है जिसकी पृथ्वी पर यह सब महिमा है यह 'आत्मा' ही है जो इस दिव्य ब्रह्मपुरी में, इस व्योम में प्रतिष्ठित है।
verses.meaning_en: The Omniscient, the All-wise, whose is this might and majesty upon the earth, is this self enthroned in the divine city of the Brahman, in his ethereal heaven.
verses.verse_skt:
यः सर्वज्ञः सर्वविद्‌ यस्यैष महिमा भुवि।
दिव्ये ब्रह्मपुरे ह्येष व्योम्न्यात्मा प्रतिष्ठितः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavid yasyaiṣa mahimā bhuvi |
divye brahmapure hyeṣa vyomnyātmā pratiṣṭhitaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरनेता  सः  हृदयम् सन्निधाय अन्ने प्रतिष्ठितः धीराः यत् आनन्दरूपम् अमृतं विभाति विज्ञानेन तत् परिपश्यन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīranetā ( saḥ ) hṛdayam sannidhāya anne pratiṣṭhitaḥ dhīrāḥ yat ānandarūpam amṛtaṁ vibhāti vijñānena tat paripaśyanti ||
verses.auro_md: A mental being, leader of the life and the body, has set a heart in matter, in matter he has taken his firm foundation. By its knowing the wise see everywhere around them That which shines in its effulgence, a shape of Bliss and Immortal.

glossary.meaning_en: leader of the life and the body
he has taken his firm foundation
a shape of Bliss and immortal
That which shines in its effulgence

verses.hindi_explain: वह मनोमय है, प्राण एवं शरीर का नेता है, जिसने जड़तत्त्व (अन्न) में हृदय को स्थापित कर दिया है, जो स्वयं जड़तत्त्व (अन्न) में सुप्रतिष्ठित है। उसे जानने से ज्ञानीजन (धीर पुरुष) अपने चारों ओर 'उस' (आत्मतत्त्व) का दर्शन करते हैं जिसकी ज्योति सर्वत्र भासित होती है, जो 'आनन्दरूप' एवं अमृत-स्वरूप है।
verses.meaning_en: A mental being, leader of the life and the body, has set a heart in matter, in matter he has taken his firm foundation. By its knowing the wise see everywhere around them That which shines in its effulgence, a shape of Bliss and immortal.
verses.verse_skt:
मनोमयः प्राणशरीरनेता प्रतिष्ठितोऽन्ने हृदयं सन्निधाय।
तद्‌ विज्ञानेन परिपश्यन्ति धीरा आनन्दरूपममृतं यद्‌ विभाति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīranetā pratiṣṭhito'nne hṛdayaṁ sannidhāya |
tad vijñānena paripaśyanti dhīrā ānandarūpamamṛtaṁ yad vibhāti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तस्मिन् परावरे दृष्टे  अस्य हृदयग्रन्थि भिद्यते  सर्वसंशयाः छिद्यन्ते कर्माणि क्षीयन्ते च ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tasmin parāvare dṛṣṭe  asya hṛdayagranthi bhidyate  sarvasaṁśayāḥ chidyante karmāṇi kṣīyante ca ||
verses.auro_md: The knot of the heart strings is rent, cut away are all doubts, and a man's works are spent and perish, when is seen That which is at once the being below and the Supreme.

glossary.meaning_en: that which is at once the being below and the Supreme

verses.hindi_explain: हृदय की सारी ग्रथियाँ खुल जाती हैं, समस्त संशय छिन्न-भिन्न हो जाते हैं, तथा मनुष्य के कर्मों का क्षय हो जाता है, जब उस 'परतत्त्व' का दर्शन हो जाता है, जो एक साथ ही अपरा सत्ता एवं 'परम सत्ता' है।
verses.meaning_en: The knot of the heartstrings is rent, cut away are all doubts, and a man's works are spent and perish, when is seen That which is at once the being below and the Supreme.
verses.verse_skt:
भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः।
क्शीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्‌ दृष्टे परावरे ॥
verses.verse_tl:
bhidyate hṛdayagranthiśchidyante sarvasaṁśayāḥ |
kśīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmin dṛṣṭe parāvare ||

verses.anvaya_skt: हिरण्मये परे कोशे विरजं निष्कलं ब्रह्म  वर्तते  तत् शुभ्रं  तत्  ज्योतिषां ज्योतिः  आत्मविदः यत् विदुः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: hiraṇmaye pare kośe virajaṁ niṣkalaṁ brahma ( vartate ) tat śubhraṁ ( tat ) jyotiṣāṁ jyotiḥ  ātmavidaḥ yat viduḥ ||
verses.auro_md: In a supreme golden sheath the Brahman lies, stainless, without parts. A Splendour is That, It is the Light of Lights, It is That which the self-knowers know.

glossary.meaning_en: It is the Light of Lights

verses.hindi_explain: उस परम हिरण्मय कोश में निष्कलंक, निरवयव 'ब्रह्म' निवास करता है 'वह' 'शुभ्र-भास्वर' है, वह 'ज्योतियों' की 'ज्योति' है, 'वही' है जिसे आत्म-ज्ञानी जानते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: In a supreme golden sheath the Brahman lies, stainless, without parts. A Splendour is That, It is the Light of Lights, It is That which the self-knowers know.
verses.verse_skt:
हिरण्मये परे कोशे विरजं ब्रह्म निष्कलम्‌।
तच्छुभ्रं ज्योतिषां ज्योतिस्तद्‌ यदात्मविदो विदुः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
hiraṇmaye pare kośe virajaṁ brahma niṣkalam |
tacchubhraṁ jyotiṣāṁ jyotistad yadātmavido viduḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तत्र सूर्यः न भाति  चन्द्रतारकम्  च  न  इमाः विद्युतः न भान्ति  अयं अग्निः कुतः  तत् प्रकाशयेत्  तं भान्तम् एव सर्वम् अनुभाति। तस्य भासा इदं सर्वम् विभाति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tatra sūryaḥ na bhāti  candratārakam ( ca ) na  imāḥ vidyutaḥ na bhānti  ayaṁ agniḥ kutaḥ ( tat prakāśayet ) taṁ bhāntam eva sarvam anubhāti| tasya bhāsā idaṁ sarvam vibhāti ||
verses.auro_md: There the sun shines not and the moon has no splendour and the stars are blind; there these lightnings flash not, how then shall burn this earthly fire? All that shines is but the shadow of his shining; all this universe is effulgent with his light.

glossary.meaning_en: and the moon has no splendour and the stars are blind
all that is shines after that

verses.hindi_explain: वहां न सूर्य प्रकाशित होता है और चन्द्र आभाहीन हो जाता है तथा तारे बुझ जाते हैं; वहां ये विद्युत् भी नहीं चमकतीं, तब यह पार्थिव अग्नि भी कैसे जल पायेगी? जो कुछ भी चमकता है, वह उसकी आभा से अनुभासित होता है, यह सम्पूर्ण विश्व उसी के प्रकाश से प्रकाशित एवं भासित हो रहा है।
verses.meaning_en: There the sun shines not and the moon has no splendour and the stars are blind; there these lightnings flash not, how then shall burn this earthly fire? All that shines is but the shadow of his shining; all this universe is effulgent with his light.
verses.verse_skt:
न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः।
तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
na tatra sūryo bhāti na candratārakaṁ nemā vidyuto bhānti kuto'yamagniḥ |
tameva bhāntamanubhāti sarvaṁ tasya bhāsā sarvamidaṁ vibhāti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: इदम् अमृतं ब्रह्म एव  ब्रह्म पुरस्तात्  ब्रह्म पश्चात्  ब्रह्म दक्षिणतः  उत्तरेण च  अधः ऊर्ध्वं च प्रसृतम्  इदं विश्वं वरिष्ठम्  ब्रह्म एव ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: idam amṛtaṁ brahma eva  brahma purastāt  brahma paścāt  brahma dakṣiṇataḥ  uttareṇa ca  adhaḥ ūrdhvaṁ ca prasṛtam  idaṁ viśvaṁ variṣṭham  brahma eva ||
verses.auro_md:
All this is Brahman immortal, naught else; Brahman is in front of us. Brahman behind us, and to the south of us and to the north of us[^4] and below us and above us; it stretches everywhere. All this is Brahman alone, all this magnificent universe.

[^4]: Or, "to the right and the left of us".

glossary.meaning_en: all this is Brahman immortal, naught else
Brahman is in front of us
and to the south of us is Brahman
and to the north of us is Brahman

verses.hindi_explain: यह सब कुछ अमृतस्वरूप 'ब्रह्म' ही है, इसके अतिरिक्त अन्य कुछ नहीं; 'ब्रह्म' ही हमारे सम्मुख है, 'ब्रह्म' ही पश्चात् है, हमारे दक्षिण में भी हमारे उत्तर में भी<sup>४</sup>, हमारे नीचे तथा हमारे ऊपर भी, यह सर्वत्र व्याप्त है। यह सम्पूर्ण अद्भुत विश्व केवल 'ब्रह्म' ही है।
verses.meaning_en: All this is Brahman immortal, naught else; Brahman is in front of us, Brahman behind us, and to the south of us and to the north of us4 and below us and above us; it stretches everywhere. All this is Brahman alone, all this magnificent universe.
verses.verse_skt:
ब्रह्मैवेदममृतं पुरस्ताद्‌ ब्रह्म पश्चाद्‌ ब्रह्म दक्शिणतश्चोत्तरेण।
अधश्चोर्ध्वं च प्रसृतं ब्रह्मैवेदं विश्वमिदं वरिष्ठम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
brahmaivedamamṛtaṁ purastād brahma paścād brahma dakśiṇataścottareṇa |
adhaścordhvaṁ ca prasṛtaṁ brahmaivedaṁ viśvamidaṁ variṣṭham ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सयुजा सखाया द्वा सुपर्णा समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते। तयोः अन्यः पिप्पलं स्वादु अत्ति। अन्यः  तु  अनश्नन् अभिचाकशीति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: sayujā sakhāyā dvā suparṇā samānaṁ vṛkṣaṁ pariṣasvajāte| tayoḥ anyaḥ pippalaṁ svādu atti| anyaḥ ( tu ) anaśnan abhicākaśīti ||
verses.auro_md: Two birds, beautiful of wing, close companions, cling to one common tree: of the two one eats the sweet fruit of the tree, the other eats not but watches his fellow.

glossary.meaning_en: the sweet fruit of the tree

verses.hindi_explain: दो सुन्दर पंखों वाले पक्षी, घनिष्ठ सखा, समान वृक्ष पर ही रहते हैं; उनमें से एक वृक्ष के स्वादिष्ट फलों को खाता है, अन्य खाता नहीं अपितु अपने सखा को देखता है।
verses.meaning_en: Two birds, beautiful of wing, close companions, cling to one common tree: of the two one eats the sweet fruit of the tree, the other eats not but watches his fellow.
verses.verse_skt:
द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते।
तयोरन्यः  पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṁ vṛkṣaṁ pariṣasvajāte |
tayoranyaḥ  pippalaṁ svādvattyanaśnannanyo abhicākaśīti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: समाने वृक्षे निमग्नः पुरुषः मुह्यमानः अनीशया शोचति हि।  सः  यदा अन्यं जुष्टम् ईशम् अस्य इति महिमानम् पश्यति  तदा सः  वीतशोकः  भवति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: samāne vṛkṣe nimagnaḥ puruṣaḥ muhyamānaḥ anīśayā śocati hi| ( saḥ ) yadā anyaṁ juṣṭam īśam asya iti mahimānam paśyati ( tadā saḥ ) vītaśokaḥ ( bhavati ) ||
verses.auro_md: The soul is the bird that sits immersed on the one common tree; but because he is not lord he is bewildered and has sorrow. But when he sees that other who is the Lord and beloved, he knows that all is His greatness and his sorrow passes away from him.

glossary.meaning_en: the soul, that is the bird
(he knows that all is) His greatness
and his sorrow passes away from him

verses.hindi_explain:
'पुरुष' (आत्मा) ही वह पक्षी है जो समान वृक्ष पर बैठकर (आस्वाद लेने में) निमग्न है; क्योंकि वह 'अनीश' है (स्वयं का ईश नहीं है) वह मोह के वशीभूत होकर शोक करता है। किन्तु जब वह उस अन्य को देखता है जो 'ईश' है, प्रेमी है, तब वह जान जाता है कि जो कुछ भी है, वह 'उसकी' महिमा है, और वह शोकमुक्त हो जाता है। 
<sup>४</sup>अथवा, "हमारे दायें भी और बायें भी।''
verses.meaning_en: The soul is the bird that sits immersed on the one common tree; but because he is not lord he is bewildered and has sorrow. But when he sees that other who is the Lord and beloved, he knows that all is His greatness and his sorrow passes away from him.
verses.verse_skt:
समाने वृक्षे पुरुषो निमग्नोऽनिशया शोचति मुह्यमानः।
जुष्टं यदा पश्यत्यन्यमीशमस्य महिमानमिति वीतशोकः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
samāne vṛkṣe puruṣo nimagno'niśayā śocati muhyamānaḥ |
juṣṭaṁ yadā paśyatyanyamīśamasya mahimānamiti vītaśokaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यदा पश्यः रुक्मवर्णं ब्रह्मयोनिम्   कर्तारम् ईशं पुरुषं पश्यते  तदा पुण्यपापे विधूय विद्वान् निरञ्जनः परमं साम्यम् उषैति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yadā paśyaḥ rukmavarṇaṁ brahmayonim   kartāram īśaṁ puruṣaṁ paśyate  tadā puṇyapāpe vidhūya vidvān nirañjanaḥ paramaṁ sāmyam uṣaiti ||
verses.auro_md:
When, a seer, he sees the Golden-hued, the maker, the Lord, the Spirit who is the source of Brahman,[^5] then he becomes the knower and shakes from his wings sin and virtue; pure of all stain he reaches the supreme identity.[^6]

[^5]: Or, "whose source is Brahman"; Shankara admits the other meaning as an alternative, but explains it as "the source of the lower Brahman".

[^6]: Or, "pure of all staining tinge he reaches to a supreme equality."

glossary.meaning_en: who is the source of Brahman

verses.hindi_explain: जब द्रष्टा उस 'स्वर्णवर्ण', कर्ता 'ईश', 'पुरुष' को देखता है जो 'ब्रह्म' की योनि<sup>५</sup> है तब वह ज्ञाता बनकर अपने पंखों से पाप एवं पुण्य को झटकार कर, 'निरंजन', निष्कलंक होकर परम तादात्म्य (साम्य) का<sup>६</sup> लाभ करता है।
verses.meaning_en: When, a seer, he sees the Golden-hued, the maker, the Lord, the Spirit who is the source of Brahman, then he becomes the knower and shakes from his wings sin and virtue; pure of all stain he reaches the supreme identity.
verses.verse_skt:
यदा पश्यः पश्यते रुक्मवर्णं कर्तारमीशं पुरुषं ब्रह्मयोनिम्‌।
तदा विद्वान्‌ पुण्यपापे विधूय निरञ्जनः परमं साम्यमुपैति ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yadā paśyaḥ paśyate rukmavarṇaṁ kartāramīśaṁ puruṣaṁ brahmayonim |
tadā vidvān puṇyapāpe vidhūya nirañjanaḥ paramaṁ sāmyamupaiti ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यः हि प्राणः एषः सर्वभूतैः विभाति   इति  विजानम् विद्वान् भवते  अतिवादी न  भवते । एषः आत्मक्रीडः आत्मरतिः क्रियावान् ब्रह्मविदां वरिष्ठः ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yaḥ hi prāṇaḥ eṣaḥ sarvabhūtaiḥ vibhāti  ( iti ) vijānam vidvān bhavate  ativādī na ( bhavate )| eṣaḥ ātmakrīḍaḥ ātmaratiḥ kriyāvān brahmavidāṁ variṣṭhaḥ ||
verses.auro_md: This is the life in things that shines manifested by all these beings; a man of knowledge coming wholly to know this, draws back from creeds and too much disputings. In the Self his delight, at play in the Self, doing works,—the best is he among the knowers of the Eternal.

glossary.meaning_en: draws back from creeds and too much disputings
among the knowers of the Eternal

verses.hindi_explain: यह प्राण ही है जो समस्त भूतपदार्थों के द्वारा अभिव्यक्त होकर उद्भासित होता है; विद्वान् व्यक्ति इसे पूर्णतया जानते हुए, मतमतान्तर एवं विवादों (अतिवाद) से बचता है। 'आत्मा' में उसकी रति होती है तथा 'आत्मा' में ही क्रीड़ारत रहकर कर्म करता रहता है,-ब्रह्मवेत्ताओं में वह सर्वश्रेष्ठः वरिष्ठ है।
verses.meaning_en: This is the life in things that shines manifested by all these beings; a man of knowledge coming wholly to know this, draws back from creeds and too much disputings. In the Self his delight, at play in the Self, doing works,-the best is he among the knowers of the Eternal.
verses.verse_skt:
प्राणो ह्येष यः सर्वभूतैर्विभाति विजानन्‌ विद्वान्‌ भवते नातिवादी।
आत्मक्रीड आत्मरतिः क्रियावानेष ब्रह्मविदां वरिष्ठः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
prāṇo hyeṣa yaḥ sarvabhūtairvibhāti vijānan vidvān bhavate nātivādī |
ātmakrīḍa ātmaratiḥ kriyāvāneṣa brahmavidāṁ variṣṭhaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यं क्षीणदोषाः यतयः पश्यन्ति  एषः ज्योतिर्मयः शुभ्रः आत्मा अन्तःशरीरे हि  सः  नित्यम् सत्येन तपसा सम्यग्ज्ञानेन ब्रह्मचर्येण  च  लभ्यः हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yaṁ kṣīṇadoṣāḥ yatayaḥ paśyanti  eṣaḥ jyotirmayaḥ śubhraḥ ātmā antaḥśarīre hi ( saḥ ) nityam satyena tapasā samyagjñānena brahmacaryeṇa ( ca ) labhyaḥ hi ||
verses.auro_md: The Self can always be won by truth, by self-discipline, by integral knowledge, by a life of purity,—this Self that is in the inner body, radiant, made all of light whom, by the perishing of their blemishes, the doers of askesis behold.

glossary.meaning_en: by the perishing of their blemishes
that is in the inner body

verses.hindi_explain: यह 'आत्मा' सत्य से, आत्म-संयम (तप) से, सम्पूर्ण एवं सम्यक् ज्ञान से, ब्रह्मचर्य से सर्वदा लभ्य है यह 'आत्मा' जो अन्तःशरीर में है शुभ एवं ज्योतिर्मय है जिसे वे तपस्वीजन (यतिजन) देखते हैं जो दोषमुक्त हो चुके हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The Self can always be won by truth, by self-discipline, by integral knowledge, by a life of purity,-this Self that is in the inner body, radiant, made all of light whom by the perishing of their blemishes the doers of askesis behold.
verses.verse_skt:
सत्येन लभ्यस्तपसा ह्येष आत्मा सम्यग्ज्ञानेन ब्रह्मचर्येण नित्यम्‌।
अन्तःशरीरे ज्योतिर्मयो हि शुभ्रो यं पश्यन्ति यतयः क्षीणदोषाः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
satyena labhyastapasā hyeṣa ātmā samyagjñānena brahmacaryeṇa nityam |
antaḥśarīre jyotirmayo hi śubhro yaṁ paśyanti yatayaḥ kṣīṇadoṣāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सत्यम् एव जयते  अनृतं न सत्येन देवयानः पन्थाः विततः। येन आप्तकामाः ऋषयः आक्रमन्ति हि  यत्र सत्यस्य तत् परमम् निधानम्  अस्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: satyam eva jayate anṛtaṁ na satyena devayānaḥ panthāḥ vitataḥ| yena āptakāmāḥ ṛṣayaḥ ākramanti hi yatra satyasya tat paramam nidhānam ( asti ) ||
verses.auro_md: It is Truth that conquers and not falsehood; by Truth was stretched out the path of the journey of the gods, by which the sages winning their desire ascend there where Truth has its Supreme abode.

glossary.meaning_en: the path of the journey of the gods

verses.hindi_explain:
'सत्य' की ही विजय होती है असत्य की नहीं; 'सत्य' के द्वारा ही देवों का यात्रा-पथ विस्तीर्ण हुआ, जिसके द्वारा आप्तकाम ऋषिगण वहां आरोहण करते हैं जहाँ 'सत्य' का परम धाम है।
<sup>५</sup>अथवा, जिसकी योनि है 'ब्रह्म' '', शंकर ने दूसरे अर्थ को एक विकल्प के रूप में स्वीकार किया है, किन्तु इसकी व्याख्या की है ''अपर ब्रह्म की योनि है।'' 
<sup>६</sup>अथवा, ''वह निष्कलंक होकर परम समत्व को प्राप्त करता है।
verses.meaning_en: It is Truth that conquers and not falsehood; by Truth was stretched out the path of the journey of the gods, by which the sages winning their desire ascend there where Truth has its supreme abode.
verses.verse_skt:
सत्यमेव जयते नानृतं सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः।
येनाक्रमन्त्यृषयो ह्याप्तकामा यत्र तत्‌ सत्यस्य परमं निधानम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
satyameva jayate nānṛtaṁ satyena panthā vitato devayānaḥ |
yenākramantyṛṣayo hyāptakāmā yatra tat satyasya paramaṁ nidhānam ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तत् बृहत् दिव्यम्  अचिन्त्यरूपं च बिभाति। तत् सूक्ष्मात् सूक्ष्मतरं च तत् दूरात् सुदूरे इह अन्तिके  इह एव पश्यत्सु गुहायां निहितं च ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: tat bṛhat divyam  acintyarūpaṁ ca bibhāti| tat sūkṣmāt sūkṣmataraṁ ca tat dūrāt sudūre iha antike  iha eva paśyatsu guhāyāṁ nihitaṁ ca ||
verses.auro_md:
Vast is That, divine, its form unthinkable; it shines out subtler than the subtle:[^7] very far and farther than farness, it is here close to us, for those who have vision it is even here in this world; it is here, hidden in the secret heart.

[^7]: Or, "minuter than the minute".

glossary.meaning_en: very far and farther than farness
it is here close to us
it is even here in this world

verses.hindi_explain: वह 'परतत्त्व' अतिविस्तृत (वृहत्) है, दिव्य है, इसका रूप अचिन्त्य है; यह सूक्ष्म से भी सूक्ष्मतर आभासित होता हैː दूरता से भी दूरतर अतिदूर है, यह यही हमारे अतिसमीप है, सत्यद्रष्टाओं के लिए वह यहीं, इसी जगत् में है; यह यहीं हृद्गुहा में निहित है।
verses.meaning_en: Vast is That, divine, its form unthinkable; it shines out subtler than the subtle: very far and farther than farness, it is here close to us, for those who have vision it is even here in this world; it is here, hidden in the secret heart.
verses.verse_skt:
बृहच्च तद्‌ दिव्यमचिन्त्यरूपं सूक्ष्माच्च तत्‌ सूक्ष्मतरं विभाति।
दूरात्‌ सुदूरे तदिहान्तिके च पश्यन्त्विहैव निहितं गुहायाम्‌ ॥
verses.verse_tl:
bṛhacca tad divyamacintyarūpaṁ sūkṣmācca tat sūkṣmataraṁ vibhāti |
dūrāt sudūre tadihāntike ca paśyantvihaiva nihitaṁ guhāyām ||

verses.anvaya_skt: तत् आत्मतत्वं  चक्षुषा न गृह्यते। वाचा अपि न। अनैः देवैः न। तपसा कर्मणा वा  न  ज्ञानप्रसादेन विशुद्धसत्वः   भवति  ततः तु ध्यायमानः निष्कलं तं पश्यते ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: ( tat ātmatatvaṁ ) cakṣuṣā na gṛhyate| vācā api na| anaiḥ devaiḥ na| tapasā karmaṇā vā ( na ) jñānaprasādena viśuddhasatvaḥ (  bhavati ) tataḥ tu dhyāyamānaḥ niṣkalaṁ taṁ paśyate ||
verses.auro_md: Eye cannot seize, speech cannot grasp Him, nor these other godheads; not by austerity can he be held nor by works: only when the inner being is purified by a glad serenity of knowledge, then indeed, meditating, one beholds the Spirit indivisible.

glossary.meaning_en: nor by these other godheads it is seized
not by austerity can he be held nor by works
by a glad serenity of knowledge
only when the inner being is purified

verses.hindi_explain: 'उसे' न चक्षु ग्रहण कर पाते हैं न वाणी, न अन्य देवता उसे ग्रहण कर पाते हैं; उसे न तपस्या से ग्रहण किया जा सकता है न कर्मों के द्वाराː जब अन्तर सत्ता ज्ञान के प्रसाद से विशुद्ध हो जाती है, केवल तभी ध्यान की अवस्था में उस अखण्ड 'परमात्म तत्त्व' को देखा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: Eye cannot seize, speech cannot grasp Him, nor these other godheads; not by austerity can he be held nor by works: only when the inner being is purified by a glad serenity of knowledge, then indeed, meditating, one beholds the Spirit indivisible.
verses.verse_skt:
न चक्षुषा गृह्यते नापि वाचा नान्यैर्देवैस्तपसा कर्मण वा।
ज्ञानप्रसादेन विशुद्धसत्त्वस्ततस्तु तं पश्यते निष्कलं ध्यायमानः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
na cakṣuṣā gṛhyate nāpi vācā nānyairdevaistapasā karmaṇa vā |
jñānaprasādena viśuddhasattvastatastu taṁ paśyate niṣkalaṁ dhyāyamānaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: एषः अणुः आत्मा चेतसा  तस्मिन् शरीरे  वेदितव्यः  यस्मिन् प्राणः पञ्चधा संविवेश। प्राणैः प्रजानाम् सर्वं चित्तम् ओतं  यस्मिन् विशुद्धे एषः आत्मा विभवति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: eṣaḥ aṇuḥ ātmā cetasā ( tasmin śarīre ) veditavyaḥ  yasmin prāṇaḥ pañcadhā saṁviveśa| prāṇaiḥ prajānām sarvaṁ cittam otaṁ  yasmin viśuddhe eṣaḥ ātmā vibhavati ||
verses.auro_md:
This self is subtle and has to be known by a thought-mind into which the life-force has made its fivefold entry: all the conscious heart of creatures is shot through and inwoven with the currents of the life-force and only when it is purified can this Self manifest its power.[^8]

[^8]: The verb *vibhavati* seems here to have a complex sense and to mean, "to manifest its full power and pervading presence".

glossary.meaning_en: with the currents of the life-force

verses.hindi_explain: यह 'आत्मा' अणुवत् सूक्ष्म है तथा इसे विचारात्मक मन (चित्त) के द्वारा जाना जाता है जिसमें प्राण ने पञ्चविध प्रवेश किया हैː प्राणियों का सचेतन मन (चित्त) प्राणों से ओत-प्रोत है जिसके विशुद्ध हो जाने पर ही यह 'आत्मा' अपनी शक्ति को अभिव्यक्त कर सकती है।<sup>८</sup>
verses.meaning_en: This Self is subtle and has to be known by a thought-mind into which the life-force has made its fivefold entry: all the conscious heart of creatures is shot through and inwoven with the currents of the life-force and only when it is purified can this Self manifest its power.
verses.verse_skt:
एषोऽणुरात्मा चेतसा वेदितव्यो यस्मिन्‌ प्राणः पञ्चधा संविवेश।
प्राणैश्चित्तं सर्वमोतं प्रजानां यस्मिन्‌ विशुद्धे विभवत्येष आत्मा ॥
verses.verse_tl:
eṣo'ṇurātmā cetasā veditavyo yasmin prāṇaḥ pañcadhā saṁviveśa |
prāṇaiścittaṁ sarvamotaṁ prajānāṁ yasmin viśuddhe vibhavatyeṣa ātmā ||

verses.anvaya_skt: विशुद्धसत्त्वः यं यं लोकं मनसा सम्बिभाति  यान् कामान् कामयते च  तं तं लोकं तान् कामान् च जयते तस्मात् भूतिकामः आत्मज्ञं अर्चयेत् हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: viśuddhasattvaḥ yaṁ yaṁ lokaṁ manasā sambibhāti  yān kāmān kāmayate ca  taṁ taṁ lokaṁ tān kāmān ca jayate tasmāt bhūtikāmaḥ ātmajñaṁ arcayet hi ||
verses.auro_md: Whatever world the man whose inner being is purified sheds the light of his mind upon, and whatsoever desires he cherishes, that world he takes by conquest, and those desires. Then, let whosoever seeks for success and well-being approach with homage a self-knower.

glossary.meaning_en: the man whose inner being is purified
sheds the light of his mind upon
let whosoever seeks for success and wellbeing

verses.hindi_explain:
जिस मनुष्य की अन्तर सत्ता विशुद्ध हो चुकी है (विशुद्धसत्त्व) वह जिस-जिस लोक पर अपने मन का प्रकाश डालता है, तथा जिन-जिन कामनाओं की वह इच्छा करता है, उस-उस लोक को वह जीत लेता है तथा उन-उन कामनाओं को जीत लेता है। अतः जो भी सफलता एवं कुशलता की अभीप्सा करता है वह आत्मज्ञानी की ही अभ्यर्चना करे। 
<sup>७</sup>अथवा, लघु से भी लघुतर।'' 
<sup>८</sup>यही 'विभवति' क्रियापद में अर्थ-वैविध्य प्रतीत होता है और उसका अर्थ है ː "अपनी सम्पूर्ण शक्ति की अभिव्यक्ति एवं सर्वव्यापी उपस्थिति।''
verses.meaning_en: Whatever world the man whose inner being is purified sheds the light of his mind upon, and whatsoever desires he cherishes, that world he takes by conquest, and those desires. Then, let whosoever seeks for success and wellbeing approach with homage a self-knower.
verses.verse_skt:
यं यं लोकं मनसा संविभाति विशुद्धसत्त्वः कामयते यांश्च कामान्‌।
तं तं लोकं जयते तांश्च कामां-स्तस्मादात्मज्ञं ह्यर्चयेत्‌ भूतिकामः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
yaṁ yaṁ lokaṁ manasā saṁvibhāti viśuddhasattvaḥ kāmayate yāṁśca kāmān |
taṁ taṁ lokaṁ jayate tāṁśca kāmāṁ-stasmādātmajñaṁ hyarcayet bhūtikāmaḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: सः एतत् परमं ब्रह्म धाम वेद  यत्र विश्वं निहितं  यं च  शुभ्रं भाति। ये अकामाः पुरुषम् उपासते  ते धीराः एतत् शुक्रं अतिवर्तन्ति हि ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: saḥ etat paramaṁ brahma dhāma veda  yatra viśvaṁ nihitaṁ ( yaṁ ca ) śubhraṁ bhāti | ye akāmāḥ puruṣam upāsate  te dhīrāḥ etat śukraṁ ativartanti hi ||
verses.auro_md:
He knows this supreme Brahman as the highest abiding place in which shines out, inset, the radiant world. The wise who are without desire and worship the Spirit pass beyond this sperm.[^9]

[^9]: Shankara takes it so in the sense of semen virile, which is the cause of birth into the cosmos. But it is possible that it means rather "pass beyond this brilliant universe", the radiant world which has just been spoken of, to the greater Light which is its abiding place and source, the supreme Brahman.
verses.hindi_explain: वह इस परम 'ब्रह्म' को परम धाम के रूप में जानता है, जिसमें ज्योतिर्मय विश्व निहित है एवं आभासित होता है, कामना से मुक्त जो विद्वान् उस 'परम पुरुष' की उपासना करते हैं, वे इस शुक्र<sup>१</sup> से परे चले जाते है।
verses.meaning_en: He knows this supreme Brahman as the highest abiding place in which shines out, inset, the radiant world. The wise who are without desire and worship the Spirit pass beyond this sperm.
verses.verse_skt:
स वेदैतत्‌ परमं ब्रह्म धाम यत्र विश्वं निहितं भाति शुभ्रम्‌।
उपासते पुरुषं ये ह्यकामास्ते शुक्रमेतदतिवर्तन्ति धीराः ॥
verses.verse_tl:
sa vedaitat paramaṁ brahma dhāma yatra viśvaṁ nihitaṁ bhāti śubhram |
upāsate puruṣaṁ ye hyakāmāste śukrametadativartanti dhīrāḥ ||

verses.anvaya_skt: यः मन्यमानः कामान् कामयते  सः कामभिः तत्र तत्र जायते। पर्याप्तकामस्य कृतात्मनः सर्वे कामाः इह एव प्रविलीयन्ति ॥
verses.anvaya_tl: yaḥ manyamānaḥ kāmān kāmayate  saḥ kāmabhiḥ tatra tatra jāyate | paryāptakāmasya kṛtātmanaḥ sarve kāmāḥ iha eva pravilīyanti ||
verses.auro_md:
He who cherishes desires and his mind dwells with his longings, is by his desires born again wherever they lead him, but the man who has won all his desire[^10] and has found his soul, for him even here in this world vanish away all desires.

[^10]: Or, "finished with desires".

glossary.meaning_en: whose mind dwells with his longings
but the man who has won all his

verses.hindi_explain: जो कामनाओं की अभिलाषा करता है तथा जिसका मन उन कामनाओं में लीन रहता है, वह उन कामनाओं के द्वारा प्रेरित वहीं-वहीं जन्म ग्रहण करता है, किन्तु जिसने अपनी कामनाओं को जीत लिया है<sup>१०</sup> तथा अपनी आत्मा को प्राप्त कर लिया है, ऐसे 'कृतात्मा' जन की यहीं, इसी लोक में समस्त कामनाएँ विलुप्त हो जाती हैं।
